897	    def record_failed_blocks(
898	        self,
899	        request_id: str,
900	        expected_mask: torch.Tensor,
901	        ret_mask: torch.Tensor,
902	        slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,
903	    ) -> set[int]:
904	        """Record block IDs associated with failed load attempts.
905	
906	        Args:
907	            request_id: request id from vLLM.
908	            expected_mask: Boolean tensor indicating which tokens were expected to
909	                be loaded from LMCache. True means the token should be loaded,
910	                False means the token is already cached in vLLM and does not need
911	                to be loaded from LMCache.
912	            ret_mask: Boolean tensor indicating which tokens were actually
913	                successfully retrieved from LMCache. True means the token was
914	                successfully loaded. For example, if 256 tokens are expected to be
915	                loaded, but only 192 tokens are successfully loaded, then the
916	                ret_mask will be a tensor of 256 items like [T, T, ..., F, F, ...]
917	                where the first 192 elements are True and the last 64 elements
918	                are False.
919	            slot_mapping: Tensor indicating slot IDs for each token. The block
920	                ID is computed by dividing the slot ID by the block size.
921	
922	        Example:
923	            expected_mask = [F, T, T, T] meaning the 1st is in vLLM cache
924	            ret_mask = [F, T, F, F] meaning failure from loading the 3rd
925	            missing_mask = expected_mask & ~ret_mask = [F, F, T, T]
926	            missing_indices = [2, 3]
927	            then missing_blocks is calculated from slot_mapping and missing_indices
928	
929	        Returns:
930	            set[int]: Set of block IDs that failed to load.
931	        """
932	
933	        if expected_mask.numel() == 0:
934	            return set()
935	
936	        expected_mask_cpu = expected_mask.to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.bool)
937	        ret_mask_cpu = ret_mask.to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.bool)
938	
939	        if ret_mask_cpu.shape[0] != expected_mask_cpu.shape[0]:
940	            logger.debug("expected_mask_cpu.shape[0] != ret_mask_cpu.shape[0]")
941	            return set()
942	
943	        missing_mask = expected_mask_cpu & ~ret_mask_cpu
944	        if not torch.any(missing_mask):
945	            return set()
946	
947	        missing_indices = torch.nonzero(missing_mask, as_tuple=False).view(-1)
948	        if missing_indices.numel() == 0:
949	            return set()
950	
951	        slot_mapping_cpu = slot_mapping.to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.long)
952	        if slot_mapping_cpu.shape[0] > missing_mask.shape[0]:
953	            slot_mapping_cpu = slot_mapping_cpu[: missing_mask.shape[0]]
954	
955	        missing_blocks_tensor = torch.unique(
956	            slot_mapping_cpu[missing_indices] // self._block_size
957	        )
958	        missing_blocks = {int(block.item()) for block in missing_blocks_tensor}
959	
960	        if not missing_blocks:
961	            return set()
962	
963	        logger.warning(
964	            "Request %s failed to load %d tokens across %d blocks",
965	            request_id,
966	            missing_indices.numel(),
967	            len(missing_blocks),
968	        )
969	        return missing_blocks
970	
971	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
972	    def wait_for_layer_load(self, layer_name: str) -> None:
973	        """Blocking until the KV for a specific layer is loaded into vLLM's
974	        paged buffer.
975	
976	        This interface will be useful for layer-by-layer pipelining.
977	
978	        Args:
979	            layer_name: the name of that layer
980	        """
981	        if self.layerwise_retrievers:
982	            logger.debug(f"Waiting for layer {self.current_layer} to be loaded")
983	
984	        # Wait for the layer to be loaded
985	        for layerwise_retriever in self.layerwise_retrievers:
986	            ret_token_mask = next(layerwise_retriever)
987	
988	            if self.current_layer == self.num_layers - 1:
989	                assert ret_token_mask is not None
990	                num_retrieved_tokens = ret_token_mask.sum().item()
991	                logger.info(f"Retrieved {num_retrieved_tokens} tokens")
992	
993	        if self.layerwise_retrievers:
994	            self.current_layer += 1
995	
996	        return

998	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
999	    def save_kv_layer(
1000	        self,
1001	        layer_name: str,
1002	        kv_layer: torch.Tensor,
1003	        attn_metadata: "AttentionMetadata",
1004	        **kwargs,
1005	    ) -> None:
1006	        """Start saving the a layer of KV cache from vLLM's paged buffer
1007	        to the connector.
1008	
1009	        Args:
1010	            layer_name (str): the name of the layer.
1011	            kv_layer (torch.Tensor): the paged KV buffer of the current
1012	                layer in vLLM.
1013	            attn_metadata (AttentionMetadata): the attention metadata.
1014	            **kwargs: additional arguments for the save operation.
1015	        """
1016	        # Degraded mode (LMCache init failed): nothing to save, fall back silently.
1017	        if self.lmcache_engine is None:
1018	            return
1019	
1020	        if not self.use_layerwise:
1021	            return
1022	
1023	        if self.kv_role == "kv_consumer":
1024	            # Don't do save if the role is kv_consumer
1025	            return
1026	        if self._parent._connector_metadata is None:
1027	            logger.warning(
1028	                "In connector.save_kv_layer, but the connector metadata is None"
1029	            )
1030	            return
1031	        connector_metadata = self._parent._get_connector_metadata()
1032	        assert isinstance(connector_metadata, LMCacheConnectorMetadata)
1033	
1034	        assert len(self.kv_caches) > 0
1035	
1036	        kvcaches = list(self.kv_caches.values())
1037	        is_first = True
1038	
1039	        for request in connector_metadata.requests:
1040	            save_spec = request.save_spec
1041	            if (
1042	                save_spec is None or not save_spec.can_save
1043	            ) and self.kv_role != "kv_producer":
1044	                continue
1045	
1046	            layerwise_storer = self._layerwise_save_storers.get(request.req_id)
1047	            if layerwise_storer is None:
1048	                token_ids = request.token_ids
1049	                assert isinstance(token_ids, list)
1050	
1051	                slot_mapping = request.slot_mapping
1052	                assert isinstance(slot_mapping, torch.Tensor)
1053	                assert len(slot_mapping) == len(token_ids)
1054	
1055	                # TODO: have a pre-allocated buffer to hold the slot_mappings
1056	                slot_mapping = slot_mapping.to(self.device)
1057	
1058	                if self.kv_role == "kv_producer":
1059	                    skip_leading_tokens = 0
1060	                else:
1061	                    assert save_spec is not None
1062	                    skip_leading_tokens = save_spec.skip_leading_tokens
1063	
1064	                    if skip_leading_tokens == len(token_ids):
1065	                        continue  # skip this request
1066	                    # Align to lmcache chunk size
1067	                    skip_leading_tokens = (
1068	                        skip_leading_tokens
1069	                        // self._lmcache_chunk_size
1070	                        * self._lmcache_chunk_size
1071	                    )
1072	
1073	                store_mask = torch.ones(len(token_ids), dtype=torch.bool)
1074	                store_mask[:skip_leading_tokens] = False
1075	
1076	                logger.debug(
1077	                    "Storing KV cache for %d out of %d tokens "
1078	                    "(skip_leading_tokens=%d) for request %s",
1079	                    len(token_ids) - skip_leading_tokens,
1080	                    len(token_ids),
1081	                    skip_leading_tokens,
1082	                    request.req_id,
1083	                )
1084	
1085	                # TODO (Jiayi): need to make layerwise storing
1086	                # compatible with disagg spec
1087	                layerwise_storer = self.lmcache_engine.store_layer(
1088	                    token_ids,
1089	                    mask=store_mask,
1090	                    kvcaches=kvcaches,
1091	                    slot_mapping=slot_mapping,
1092	                    offset=skip_leading_tokens,
1093	                    sync=is_first,
1094	                    req_id=request.req_id,
1095	                )
1096	                self._layerwise_save_storers[request.req_id] = layerwise_storer
1097	                if is_first:
1098	                    is_first = False
1099	
1100	            next(layerwise_storer)
1101	
1102	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1103	    def wait_for_save(self):
1104	        """Blocking until the KV cache is saved to the connector buffer."""
1105	
1106	        # Degraded mode (LMCache init failed): no engine to save to / unpin from.
1107	        if self.lmcache_engine is None:
1108	            return
1109	
1110	        connector_metadata = self._parent._get_connector_metadata()
1111	        assert isinstance(connector_metadata, LMCacheConnectorMetadata)
1112	
1113	        if self.kv_role == "kv_consumer":
1114	            # Don't do save if the role is kv_consumer
1115	            # But still need to unpin the kv caches according to req_id
1116	            # to balance the pin count from contains()
1117	            assert self.lmcache_engine is not None, (
1118	                "LMCacheEngine must be initialized to unpin requests."
1119	            )
1120	            for request in connector_metadata.requests:
1121	                self.lmcache_engine.lookup_unpin(request.req_id)
1122	
1123	            return
1124	
1125	        if self.use_layerwise:
1126	            for request in connector_metadata.requests:
1127	                layerwise_storer = self._layerwise_save_storers.pop(
1128	                    request.req_id, None
1129	                )
1130	                if layerwise_storer is not None:
1131	                    next(layerwise_storer)
1132	                # unpin the kv caches according to req_id
1133	                self.lmcache_engine.lookup_unpin(request.req_id)
1134	            return
1135	
1136	        assert len(self.kv_caches) > 0
1137	        kvcaches = list(self.kv_caches.values())
1138	
1139	        assert self.lmcache_engine is not None
1140	
1141	        # Probe decoder cache before store if bidirectional mode is enabled
1142	        bidir_enabled = getattr(self.config, "pd_bidirectional", False)
1143	
1144	        for request in connector_metadata.requests:
1145	            # unpin the kv caches according to req_id
1146	            self.lmcache_engine.lookup_unpin(request.req_id)
1147	
1148	            save_spec = request.save_spec
1149	            if (
1150	                save_spec is None or not save_spec.can_save
1151	            ) and self.kv_role != "kv_producer":
1152	                continue
1153	
1154	            token_ids = request.token_ids
1155	
1156	            slot_mapping = request.slot_mapping
1157	            assert isinstance(slot_mapping, torch.Tensor)
1158	            if len(slot_mapping) != len(token_ids):
1159	                logger.warning(
1160	                    "Skipping KV save for request %s: slot_mapping/token_ids "
1161	                    "length mismatch (slot_mapping=%d, token_ids=%d). Likely "
1162	                    "an upstream allocation/preemption desync; the engine "
1163	                    "stays alive and only this request's save is dropped.",
1164	                    request.req_id,
1165	                    len(slot_mapping),
1166	                    len(token_ids),
1167	                )
1168	                continue
1169	
1170	            # TODO: have a pre-allocated buffer to hold the slot_mappings
1171	            slot_mapping = slot_mapping.to(self.device)
1172	
1173	            skip_leading_tokens = save_spec.skip_leading_tokens
1174	            # shared storage disaggregation will not have a disagg_spec passed in
1175	            if self.kv_role == "kv_producer" and request.disagg_spec:
1176	                skip_leading_tokens = min(
1177	                    skip_leading_tokens, request.disagg_spec.num_transferred_tokens
1178	                )
1179	
1180	            if skip_leading_tokens == len(token_ids):
1181	                continue  # skip this request
1182	            # Align to lmcache chunk size
1183	            skip_leading_tokens = (
1184	                skip_leading_tokens
1185	                // self._lmcache_chunk_size
1186	                * self._lmcache_chunk_size
1187	            )
1188	
1189	            store_mask = torch.ones(len(token_ids), dtype=torch.bool)
1190	            store_mask[:skip_leading_tokens] = False
1191	
1192	            logger.debug(
1193	                "Storing KV cache for %d out of %d tokens "
1194	                "(skip_leading_tokens=%d) for request %s",
1195	                len(token_ids) - skip_leading_tokens,
1196	                len(token_ids),
1197	                skip_leading_tokens,
1198	                request.req_id,
1199	            )
1200	
1201	            is_last_prefill = request.is_last_prefill
1202	            if is_last_prefill:
1203	                if request.disagg_spec:
1204	                    request.disagg_spec.is_last_prefill = True
1205	            else:
1206	                if not self.enable_blending:
1207	                    token_len = len(token_ids)
1208	                    aligned_token_len = (
1209	                        token_len // self._lmcache_chunk_size * self._lmcache_chunk_size
1210	                    )
1211	                    token_ids = token_ids[:aligned_token_len]
1212	                    store_mask = store_mask[:aligned_token_len]
1213	                    slot_mapping = slot_mapping[:aligned_token_len]
1214	
1215	            # Probe decoder cache before store
1216	            if bidir_enabled and request.disagg_spec is not None:
1217	                try:
1218	                    self._probe_decoder_cache(request, token_ids)
1219	                except Exception as e:
1220	                    logger.warning(
1221	                        "Bidirectional NIXL cache probe failed for %s: %s",
1222	                        request.req_id,
1223	                        e,
1224	                    )
1225	
1226	            self.lmcache_engine.store(
1227	                token_ids,
1228	                mask=store_mask,
1229	                kvcaches=kvcaches,
1230	                slot_mapping=slot_mapping,
1231	                offset=skip_leading_tokens,
1232	                transfer_spec=request.disagg_spec,
1233	                request_configs=request.request_configs,
1234	                req_id=request.req_id,
1235	            )
1236	
1237	            # Probe decoder cache after store
1238	            if (
1239	                bidir_enabled
1240	                and request.disagg_spec is not None
1241	                and request.disagg_spec.receiver_query_port is not None
1242	            ):
1243	                try:
1244	                    self._probe_decoder_cache(request, token_ids)
1245	                except Exception as e:
1246	                    logger.warning(
1247	                        "Bidirectional NIXL cache probe failed for %s: %s",
1248	                        request.req_id,
1249	                        e,
1250	                    )
1251	
1252	            # Update skip_leading_tokens only on last rank to ensure
1253	            # each PP stage stores its own KV cache
1254	            if get_pp_group().is_last_rank:
1255	                # NOTE(Jiayi): We assume all tokens are saved
1256	                save_spec.skip_leading_tokens = len(token_ids)
1257	                if request.disagg_spec:
1258	                    request.disagg_spec.num_transferred_tokens = len(token_ids)
1259	
1260	    def _probe_decoder_cache(self, request: ReqMeta, token_ids: list[int]) -> None:
1261	        """Query the decoder's cache to check which blocks are already cached.
1262	
1263	        This is the bidirectional NIXL cache probe: the prefiller queries the
1264	        decoder via ZMQ to find out which KV blocks are already in the
1265	        decoder's GPU memory. This validates the cache query channel works
1266	        E2E through the real inference path.
1267	
1268	        In the future, this information can be used to skip prefill
1269	        computation for cached blocks.
1270	        """
1271	        sm = self.lmcache_engine.storage_manager  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1272	        if sm is None or sm.allocator_backend is None:
1273	            return
1274	        pd_backend = sm.allocator_backend
1275	        if not hasattr(pd_backend, "query_remote_cache"):
1276	            return
1277	        if not hasattr(pd_backend, "cache_query_sockets"):
1278	            return
1279	
1280	        # Get query port from LMCache config (pd_peer_query_port)
1281	        query_ports = self.config.pd_peer_query_port
1282	        if query_ports is None:
1283	            return
1284	
1285	        # Build cache keys using the token database's process_tokens
1286	        td = self.lmcache_engine.token_database  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1287	        if td is None:
1288	            return
1289	
1290	        chunk_keys = []
1291	        for _start, _end, key in td.process_tokens(
1292	            tokens=token_ids, mask=None, make_key=True
1293	        ):
1294	            chunk_keys.append(key)
1295	
1296	        if not chunk_keys:
1297	            return
1298	
1299	        # Build receiver_id from disagg_spec
1300	        disagg = request.disagg_spec
1301	        init_port = disagg.receiver_init_port  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1302	        if isinstance(init_port, list):
1303	            init_port = init_port[pd_backend.tp_rank]  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1304	        receiver_id = disagg.receiver_host + str(init_port)  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1305	
1306	        # Ensure peer and cache query connections
1307	        alloc_port = disagg.receiver_alloc_port  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1308	        if isinstance(alloc_port, list):
1309	            alloc_port = alloc_port[pd_backend.tp_rank]  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1310	        query_port = query_ports[pd_backend.tp_rank]  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1311	
1312	        pd_backend._ensure_peer_connection(  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1313	            receiver_id=receiver_id,
1314	            receiver_host=disagg.receiver_host,  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1315	            receiver_init_port=init_port,
1316	            receiver_alloc_port=alloc_port,
1317	        )
1318	        pd_backend._ensure_cache_query_connection(  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1319	            receiver_id=receiver_id,
1320	            receiver_host=disagg.receiver_host,  # type: ignore[union-attr]
1321	            receiver_query_port=query_port,
1322	        )
1323	
1324	        # Query decoder cache
1325	        cache_resp = pd_backend.query_remote_cache(receiver_id, chunk_keys)
1326	
1327	        logger.info(
1328	            "Bidirectional NIXL cache probe: req=%s, "
1329	            "queried %d chunks, decoder has %d cached "
1330	            "(%.0f%% hit rate)",
1331	            request.req_id,
1332	            len(chunk_keys),
1333	            len(cache_resp.cached_keys),
1334	            100.0 * len(cache_resp.cached_keys) / len(chunk_keys) if chunk_keys else 0,
1335	        )
1336

1344	        invalid_blocks = self._invalid_block_ids.copy()
1345	        self._invalid_block_ids.clear()

1351	        logger.info("Starting LMCacheConnector shutdown...")
1352	        self._manager.stop_services()

1358	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1359	    def get_num_new_matched_tokens(
1360	        self,
1361	        request: "Request",
1362	        num_computed_tokens: int,
1363	    ) -> Optional[int]:
1364	        """
1365	        Check for external KV cache hit.
1366	
1367	        Args:
1368	            request (Request): the request object.
1369	            num_computed_tokens (int): the number of locally
1370	                computed tokens for this request
1371	
1372	        Returns:
1373	            the number of tokens that can be loaded from the
1374	            external KV cache beyond what is already computed.
1375	        """
1376	        # Ignore DP attention mock requests
1377	        if request.request_id.startswith("mock_req"):
1378	            return 0
1379	        # to handle preempted requests, we want `get_num_new_matched_tokens` to be
1380	        # idempotent under the condition that `update_state_after_alloc` is NOT called
1381	        # then the two side-effects that must be idempotent are:
1382	        # 1. lookup_client caches a result
1383	        #     uncached in `update_state_after_alloc` if this request can be scheduled
1384	        # 2. cache engine will pin the KV caches for the request
1385	        #     unpinned in `wait_for_save` if this request can be scheduled
1386	        if self.kv_role == "kv_producer" and not hasattr(
1387	            self.lookup_client, "supports_producer_reuse"
1388	        ):
1389	            return 0
1390	
1391	        req_id = request.request_id
1392	
1393	        # Degraded mode (LMCache init failed): no lookup client is available, so
1394	        # report no external hits and let vLLM recompute instead of asserting and
1395	        # crashing EngineCore during scheduling.
1396	        if self.lookup_client is None:
1397	            return 0
1398	
1399	        if (
1400	            num_external_hit_tokens := self.lookup_client.lookup_cache(lookup_id=req_id)
1401	        ) != -1:
1402	            # -1 means no result cached
1403	            # None or int means ongoing (async) or cached result
1404	            logger.debug(
1405	                f"Found {num_external_hit_tokens} hit tokens for request"
1406	                f" {req_id} in the lookup cache."
1407	            )
1408	        else:
1409	            logger.debug(f"Looking up cache for the first time for request {req_id}!")
1410	            self._requests_priority[req_id] = getattr(request, "priority", 0)
1411	
1412	            # token_ids = request.prompt_token_ids
1413	            # all token ids covers the preemption case
1414	            token_ids = request.all_token_ids
1415	
1416	            # If the request has multimodal hashes, apply them to the token ids
1417	            mm_hashes, mm_positions = extract_mm_features(request)
1418	            if mm_hashes and mm_positions:
1419	                # TODO(Jiayi): Optimize this
1420	                token_ids = torch.tensor(request.prompt_token_ids)
1421	                apply_mm_hashes_to_token_ids(token_ids, mm_hashes, mm_positions)
1422	                token_ids = token_ids.tolist()
1423	
1424	            request_configs = extract_request_configs(request.sampling_params)
1425	            if self.skip_last_n_tokens > 0:
1426	                token_ids = token_ids[: -self.skip_last_n_tokens]
1427	
1428	            num_external_hit_tokens = self.lookup_client.lookup(
1429	                token_ids,
1430	                lookup_id=req_id,
1431	                request_configs=request_configs,
1432	            )
1433	
1434	        if num_external_hit_tokens is None:
1435	            logger.debug(
1436	                "Reqid: %s, Total tokens %d, Inference Engine computed tokens: %d, "
1437	                "LMCache hit tokens: None.",
1438	                req_id,
1439	                request.num_tokens,
1440	                num_computed_tokens,
1441	            )
1442	            return None
1443	
1444	        # When prompt length is divisible by the block size and all
1445	        # blocks are cached, we need to recompute the last token.
1446	        # This will be removed in the future if vLLM's scheduler provides
1447	        # a better support for this case.
1448	        need_to_allocate = num_external_hit_tokens - num_computed_tokens
1449	
1450	        # In, full-prompt-hit case, we need to recompute the last token
1451	        if num_external_hit_tokens == request.num_tokens:
1452	            need_to_allocate -= 1
1453	
1454	        # Check if hit tokens meet the minimum for retrieve
1455	        # If below minimum, skip retrieve but still record hit tokens
1456	        # for skip_leading_tokens to avoid re-storing existing chunks
1457	        min_retrieve = self.config.min_retrieve_tokens
1458	        below_min_retrieve = min_retrieve > 0 and need_to_allocate < min_retrieve
1459	
1460	        if below_min_retrieve:
1461	            logger.info(
1462	                "Reqid: %s, Total tokens %d, Inference Engine computed tokens: %d, "
1463	                "LMCache hit tokens: %d, but need to load: %d < min_retrieve %d, "
1464	                "skip retrieve but record for save skip",
1465	                req_id,
1466	                request.num_tokens,
1467	                num_computed_tokens,
1468	                num_external_hit_tokens,
1469	                max(need_to_allocate, 0),
1470	                min_retrieve,
1471	            )
1472	        else:
1473	            logger.info(
1474	                "Reqid: %s, Total tokens %d, Inference Engine computed tokens: %d, "
1475	                "LMCache hit tokens: %d, need to load: %d",
1476	                req_id,
1477	                request.num_tokens,
1478	                num_computed_tokens,
1479	                num_external_hit_tokens,
1480	                max(need_to_allocate, 0),
1481	            )
1482	
1483	        # Chunked KV loading: cap the number of tokens reported
1484	        # to the scheduler to avoid exhausting the GPU block pool
1485	        # when many concurrent requests each need large allocations.
1486	        # Remaining tokens beyond the cap will be computed locally
1487	        # via chunked prefill rather than loaded from external cache.
1488	        capped_lmcache_tokens = num_external_hit_tokens
1489	        if (
1490	            self._max_tokens_per_load > 0
1491	            and need_to_allocate > self._max_tokens_per_load
1492	        ):
1493	            # Align cap to LMCache chunk boundaries so that the
1494	            # retrieve path receives chunk-aligned token ranges.
1495	            cap = (
1496	                self._max_tokens_per_load
1497	                // self._lmcache_chunk_size
1498	                * self._lmcache_chunk_size
1499	            )
1500	            need_to_allocate = cap
1501	            # Align capped_lmcache_tokens to chunk boundary so that
1502	            # session.get_hashes() receives a chunk-aligned end value.
1503	            capped_lmcache_tokens = (
1504	                (num_computed_tokens + cap)
1505	                // self._lmcache_chunk_size
1506	                * self._lmcache_chunk_size
1507	            )
1508	            logger.debug(
1509	                "Reqid: %s, Chunked KV loading: capped from "
1510	                "%d to %d external tokens "
1511	                "(max_tokens_per_load=%d)",
1512	                req_id,
1513	                num_external_hit_tokens - num_computed_tokens,
1514	                need_to_allocate,
1515	                self._max_tokens_per_load,
1516	            )
1517	
1518	        self.load_specs[req_id] = LoadSpec(
1519	            vllm_cached_tokens=num_computed_tokens,
1520	            lmcache_cached_tokens=capped_lmcache_tokens,
1521	            can_load=False,
1522	        )
1523	
1524	        if below_min_retrieve or need_to_allocate <= 0:
1525	            return 0
1526	
1527	        # TODO: Align to vLLM block size. Should test whether it can be removed
1528	        # need_to_allocate = need_to_allocate // self._block_size * \
1529	        #        self._block_size
1530	
1531	        return need_to_allocate
1532	
1533	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1534	    def update_state_after_alloc(self, request: "Request", num_external_tokens: int):
1535	        """
1536	        Update KVConnector state after temporary buffer alloc.
1537	
1538	        For SharedStorageConnector, update _request_needs_load
1539	        if the CacheManager this allocated blocks for us.
1540	        """
1541	
1542	        # Degraded mode (LMCache init failed): there is no lookup client to clear
1543	        # and no load spec was recorded, so nothing to do.
1544	        if self.lookup_client is None:
1545	            return
1546	
1547	        # Clear local status in lookup client when a new request is
1548	        # successfully scheduled.
1549	        self.lookup_client.clear_lookup_status(request.request_id)
1550	
1551	        kv_transfer_params = (
1552	            request.kv_transfer_params
1553	            if hasattr(request, "kv_transfer_params")
1554	            else None
1555	        )
1556	
1557	        if kv_transfer_params is not None and "disagg_spec" in kv_transfer_params:
1558	            req_disagg_spec = kv_transfer_params["disagg_spec"]
1559	
1560	            receiver_id = req_disagg_spec["receiver_host"] + str(
1561	                req_disagg_spec["receiver_init_port"]
1562	            )
1563	
1564	            disagg_spec = DisaggSpec(
1565	                req_id=req_disagg_spec["req_id"],
1566	                receiver_id=receiver_id,
1567	                receiver_host=req_disagg_spec["receiver_host"],
1568	                receiver_init_port=req_disagg_spec["receiver_init_port"],
1569	                receiver_alloc_port=req_disagg_spec["receiver_alloc_port"],
1570	                receiver_query_port=req_disagg_spec.get("receiver_query_port"),
1571	            )
1572	
1573	            tmp_disagg_tracker[request.request_id] = disagg_spec
1574	        self._unfinished_requests[request.request_id] = request
1575	
1576	        if request.request_id not in self.load_specs:
1577	            # No KV tokens from external KV cache, return
1578	            return
1579	
1580	        if num_external_tokens == 0:
1581	            # No need to load anything
1582	            self.load_specs[request.request_id].can_load = False
1583	            return
1584	
1585	        recalc_last = (
1586	            1
1587	            if (
1588	                self.load_specs[request.request_id].lmcache_cached_tokens
1589	                == request.num_tokens
1590	            )
1591	            else 0
1592	        )
1593	        assert (
1594	            num_external_tokens
1595	            == self.load_specs[request.request_id].lmcache_cached_tokens
1596	            - self.load_specs[request.request_id].vllm_cached_tokens
1597	            - recalc_last
1598	        ), (
1599	            f"Mismatch in tokens to load: {num_external_tokens} vs "
1600	            f"{self.load_specs[request.request_id].lmcache_cached_tokens} "
1601	            "(tokens in lmcache) - "
1602	            f"{self.load_specs[request.request_id].vllm_cached_tokens} "
1603	            "(tokens in vllm) - "
1604	            f"{recalc_last} "
1605	            "(full lmcache hits subtracts last token to recalculate logits)"
1606	            f" for request {request.request_id}"
1607	        )
1608	
1609	        self.load_specs[request.request_id].can_load = True

1611	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1612	    def build_connector_meta(
1613	        self, scheduler_output: SchedulerOutput
1614	    ) -> KVConnectorMetadata:
1615	        """Attach the connector metadata to the request object.
1616	
1617	        This function should NOT modify other fields in the scheduler_output
1618	        except the `kv_connector_metadata` field.
1619	        Also, calling this function will reset the state of the connector.
1620	
1621	        Args:
1622	            scheduler_output (SchedulerOutput): the scheduler output object.
1623	        """
1624	
1625	        # Degraded mode (LMCache init failed): no lookup client means no load specs
1626	        # were ever recorded, so return empty metadata for the worker-side hooks.
1627	        if self.lookup_client is None:
1628	            return LMCacheConnectorMetadata()
1629	
1630	        force_skip_save = self.kv_role == "kv_consumer" or self.force_skip_save
1631	
1632	        meta = LMCacheConnectorMetadata()
1633	
1634	        for finished_req_id in scheduler_output.finished_req_ids:
1635	            self._request_trackers.pop(finished_req_id, None)
1636	            self._unfinished_requests.pop(finished_req_id, None)
1637	
1638	        # We should load KV for:
1639	        # 1. new requests
1640	        # 2. preempted requests (once per recovery)
1641	        # can_load will only be True if `update_state_after_alloc` has been called
1642	        # which only happens when vLLM's KV manager has space to receive KV from LMCache
1643	        for request in scheduler_output.scheduled_new_reqs:
1644	            # Ignore DP attention mock requests
1645	            if request.req_id.startswith("mock_req"):
1646	                continue
1647	            load_spec = self.load_specs.pop(request.req_id, None)
1648	            num_tokens_to_compute = (
1649	                request.num_computed_tokens
1650	                + scheduler_output.num_scheduled_tokens[request.req_id]
1651	            )
1652	            lmcache_cached_tokens = 0
1653	            if load_spec is not None:
1654	                lmcache_cached_tokens = load_spec.lmcache_cached_tokens
1655	            request_priority = self._requests_priority.pop(request.req_id, 0)
1656	
1657	            skip_save = force_skip_save or (
1658	                self.config.priority_limit is not None
1659	                and request_priority > self.config.priority_limit
1660	            )
1661	
1662	            request_tracker = RequestTracker.from_new_request(
1663	                self.config,
1664	                request,
1665	                num_tokens_to_compute,
1666	                lmcache_cached_tokens,
1667	                skip_save,
1668	            )
1669	            self._request_trackers[request.req_id] = request_tracker
1670	
1671	            req_meta = ReqMeta.from_request_tracker(
1672	                request_tracker,
1673	                self._block_size,
1674	                self._lmcache_chunk_size,
1675	                load_spec=load_spec,
1676	                discard_partial_chunks=self._discard_partial_chunks,
1677	                save_decode_cache=self.config.save_decode_cache,
1678	            )
1679	            if req_meta is not None:
1680	                meta.add_request(req_meta)
1681	
1682	        cached_reqs = scheduler_output.scheduled_cached_reqs
1683	
1684	        # NOTE: For backward compatibility with vllm version < 0.9.2,
1685	        # In the latest vllm version, the type of scheduled_cached_reqs has
1686	        # changed from list to object `CachedRequestData`
1687	        if isinstance(cached_reqs, list):
1688	            for i, req in enumerate(cached_reqs):
1689	                load_spec = self.load_specs.pop(req.req_id, None)
1690	                lmcache_cached_tokens = 0
1691	                vllm_cached_tokens = 0
1692	                if load_spec is not None:
1693	                    lmcache_cached_tokens = load_spec.lmcache_cached_tokens
1694	                    vllm_cached_tokens = load_spec.vllm_cached_tokens
1695	                request_tracker = self._request_trackers[req.req_id]
1696	
1697	                # Pass all_token_ids for preempted requests to restore
1698	                # token_ids correctly for chunk key computation
1699	                all_token_ids = None
1700	                if req.resumed_from_preemption:
1701	                    vllm_request = self._unfinished_requests.get(req.req_id)
1702	                    assert vllm_request is not None, (
1703	                        f"Preempted request {req.req_id} not found "
1704	                        "in _unfinished_requests"
1705	                    )
1706	                    all_token_ids = list(vllm_request.all_token_ids)
1707	
1708	                request_tracker.update(
1709	                    req.new_token_ids,
1710	                    req.new_block_ids,
1711	                    req.resumed_from_preemption,
1712	                    lmcache_cached_tokens=lmcache_cached_tokens,
1713	                    vllm_cached_tokens=vllm_cached_tokens,
1714	                    all_token_ids=all_token_ids,
1715	                )
1716	
1717	                req_meta = ReqMeta.from_request_tracker(
1718	                    request_tracker,
1719	                    self._block_size,
1720	                    self._lmcache_chunk_size,
1721	                    load_spec=load_spec,
1722	                    discard_partial_chunks=self._discard_partial_chunks,
1723	                    save_decode_cache=self.config.save_decode_cache,
1724	                )
1725	                if req_meta is not None:
1726	                    meta.add_request(req_meta)
1727	            return meta
1728	
1729	        for i, req_id in enumerate(cached_reqs.req_ids):
1730	            request_tracker = self._request_trackers[req_id]
1731	            num_new_tokens = scheduler_output.num_scheduled_tokens[req_id]
1732	            # TODO: this is a dangerous reference to the request object inside vllm
1733	            if request := self._unfinished_requests.get(req_id):
1734	                num_current_tokens = request.num_computed_tokens
1735	                # tracker_len < num_computed_tokens during decode
1736	                #   (important for save_decode_cache).
1737	                # num_computed_tokens < tracker_len after preemption.
1738	                tracker_len = len(request_tracker.token_ids)
1739	                slice_base = min(num_current_tokens, tracker_len)
1740	                new_token_ids = request.all_token_ids[
1741	                    slice_base : slice_base + num_new_tokens
1742	                ]
1743	            else:
1744	                raise ValueError(
1745	                    f"Request {req_id} is not in _unfinished_requests, "
1746	                    f"but it is scheduled to be cached"
1747	                )
1748	            new_block_ids = cached_reqs.new_block_ids[i]
1749	
1750	            load_spec = self.load_specs.pop(req_id, None)
1751	            lmcache_cached_tokens = 0
1752	            vllm_cached_tokens = 0
1753	            if load_spec is not None:
1754	                lmcache_cached_tokens = load_spec.lmcache_cached_tokens
1755	                vllm_cached_tokens = load_spec.vllm_cached_tokens
1756	
1757	            # Handle both old and new versions of CachedRequestData
1758	            if hasattr(cached_reqs, "resumed_req_ids"):
1759	                # New version with resumed_req_ids
1760	                preempted = req_id in cached_reqs.resumed_req_ids
1761	            elif hasattr(cached_reqs, "resumed_from_preemption"):
1762	                # Old version with resumed_from_preemption
1763	                preempted = cached_reqs.resumed_from_preemption[i]
1764	            else:
1765	                # This case should not be reached with supported vLLM versions.
1766	                # Raising an error is safer than assuming not preempted.
1767	                raise AttributeError(
1768	                    f"Unable to determine preemption status for request {req_id}. "
1769	                    f"This might be due to an unsupported vLLM version."
1770	                )
1771	            if preempted:
1772	                assert load_spec is not None, (
1773	                    f"Request {req_id} is preempted but was not given a load spec"
1774	                )
1775	                # num_computed_tokens should be reset to 0 during preemption
1776	                # and then set to the number of already cached tokens (maxxing
1777	                # prefix caching and lmcache)
1778	                # this assumption is crucial for the update() call of RequestTracker
1779	                # On full cache hit, get_num_new_matched_tokens subtracts 1
1780	                # to force last-token recomputation. This only affects
1781	                # num_computed_tokens when lmcache has all tokens AND
1782	                # provides more than vLLM's local cache.
1783	                expected = max(lmcache_cached_tokens, load_spec.vllm_cached_tokens)
1784	                full_hit_adj = (
1785	                    lmcache_cached_tokens == len(request.all_token_ids)
1786	                    and lmcache_cached_tokens > load_spec.vllm_cached_tokens
1787	                )
1788	                if full_hit_adj:
1789	                    expected -= 1
1790	                assert request.num_computed_tokens == expected, (
1791	                    f"Preempted request {req_id} has "
1792	                    f"num_computed_tokens {request.num_computed_tokens} "
1793	                    f"but expected {expected} "
1794	                    f"(full_hit_adj={full_hit_adj})"
1795	                )
1796	
1797	            # When retrieve fail, vllm will call _handle_invalid_blocks to
1798	            # reset request.num_computed_tokens, this will lead to
1799	            # request_tracker.token_ids being not matched with vllm
1800	            if num_current_tokens < len(request_tracker.token_ids):
1801	                logger.warning(
1802	                    "Request %s rolled back from %d to %d tokens; "
1803	                    "truncating tracker state.",
1804	                    req_id,
1805	                    len(request_tracker.token_ids),
1806	                    num_current_tokens,
1807	                )
1808	                num_token_slots = (
1809	                    len(request_tracker.allocated_block_ids) * self._block_size
1810	                )
1811	                tokens_to_keep = num_current_tokens
1812	                if num_token_slots < num_current_tokens:
1813	                    logger.warning(
1814	                        "Request %s tracker has %d token slots but %d tokens; "
1815	                        "capping token_ids to slot capacity.",
1816	                        req_id,
1817	                        num_token_slots,
1818	                        num_current_tokens,
1819	                    )
1820	                    tokens_to_keep = num_token_slots
1821	
1822	                request_tracker.token_ids = list(request.all_token_ids[:tokens_to_keep])
1823	                request_tracker.num_saved_tokens = min(
1824	                    request_tracker.num_saved_tokens, tokens_to_keep
1825	                )
1826	
1827	            # Pass all_token_ids for preempted requests to restore
1828	            # token_ids correctly for chunk key computation
1829	            all_token_ids = list(request.all_token_ids) if preempted else None
1830	
1831	            request_tracker.update(
1832	                new_token_ids,
1833	                new_block_ids,
1834	                preempted=preempted,
1835	                lmcache_cached_tokens=lmcache_cached_tokens,
1836	                vllm_cached_tokens=vllm_cached_tokens,
1837	                all_token_ids=all_token_ids,
1838	            )
1839	
1840	            req_meta = ReqMeta.from_request_tracker(
1841	                request_tracker,
1842	                self._block_size,
1843	                self._lmcache_chunk_size,
1844	                load_spec=load_spec,
1845	                discard_partial_chunks=self._discard_partial_chunks,
1846	                save_decode_cache=self.config.save_decode_cache,
1847	            )
1848	            if req_meta is not None:
1849	                meta.add_request(req_meta)
1850	
1851	        return meta

1853	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1854	    def request_finished(
1855	        self,
1856	        request: "Request",
1857	        block_ids: list[int],
1858	    ) -> tuple[bool, Optional[dict[str, Any]]]:
1859	        # Layerwise save uses request-scoped generators. If request finishes
1860	        # without entering wait_for_save (abort/error/evict path), make sure
1861	        # we release the generator entry to avoid leaking state.
1862	        if getattr(self, "use_layerwise", False) and hasattr(
1863	            self, "_layerwise_save_storers"
1864	        ):
1865	            self._layerwise_save_storers.pop(request.request_id, None)
1866	
1867	        # Cleanup if request was aborted. The per-branch logic below already
1868	        # degrades gracefully when ``lmcache_engine`` and/or ``lookup_client``
1869	        # are missing (it warns and skips only the unavailable backend), so we
1870	        # must not short-circuit the whole method here -- doing so would drop
1871	        # the storage/lookup cancels that still need to run when just one of
1872	        # the two is present. See LMCache#3337.
1873	        if request.status == RequestStatus.FINISHED_ABORTED:
1874	            # ``request_finished`` is a Scheduler-side connector API.
1875	            # The Scheduler typically does not initialize the storage
1876	            # engine (unless ``enable_scheduler_bypass_lookup`` is set);
1877	            # only the Worker role builds it by default. The Scheduler
1878	            # *does* own the lookup_client though, so the async lookup
1879	            # cancel below must run independently of the engine check
1880	            # to avoid leaking in-flight async lookups on Scheduler-side
1881	            # aborts. See LMCache#3337.
1882	            if self.lmcache_engine is None:
1883	                logger.warning(
1884	                    "Skipping abort-time backend cleanup for request %s: "
1885	                    "lmcache_engine is not initialized (Scheduler role "
1886	                    "without enable_scheduler_bypass_lookup).",
1887	                    request.request_id,
1888	                )
1889	            else:
1890	                # Notify storage backends of aborted requests
1891	                sm = self.lmcache_engine.storage_manager
1892	                if sm is not None:
1893	                    sm.cancel_request(request.request_id)
1894	
1895	            if self.async_loading:
1896	                # Cancel any ongoing async lookup and prefetch tasks on
1897	                # workers. Independent of ``lmcache_engine`` because the
1898	                # Scheduler owns ``lookup_client`` even when it does not
1899	                # build an engine.
1900	                lookup_id = request.request_id
1901	                if self.lookup_client is None:
1902	                    logger.warning(
1903	                        "Skipping abort-time async lookup cancel for "
1904	                        "request %s: lookup_client is not initialized "
1905	                        "while async_loading is enabled. Engine stays "
1906	                        "alive; this request's lookup is dropped.",
1907	                        request.request_id,
1908	                    )
1909	                else:
1910	                    self.lookup_client.cancel_lookup(lookup_id)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
1911	
1912	        params = (
1913	            request.kv_transfer_params
1914	            if hasattr(request, "kv_transfer_params")
1915	            else None
1916	        )
1917	        return_params = None
1918	
1919	        # NOTE: Used to stream back the first token
1920	        # for disagg prefill
1921	        if params is not None and "ret_first_tok" in params:
1922	            return_params = {
1923	                "first_tok": request._output_token_ids[0],
1924	            }
1925	
1926	        if self.config.get_extra_config_value(
1927	            "enable_cache_usage_details_in_response", False
1928	        ):
1929	            request_tracker = self._request_trackers.get(request.request_id)
1930	            if request_tracker:
1931	                return_params = return_params or {}
1932	                return_params["num_lmcache_cached_tokens"] = (
1933	                    request_tracker.num_lmcache_cached_tokens
1934	                )
1935	
1936	        return False, return_params
1937	
1938	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1939	    def get_kv_events(self) -> Iterable[CacheStoreEvent]:
1940	        if self.lmcache_engine is not None:
1941	            return self.lmcache_engine.get_kv_events()
