40	logger = init_logger(__name__)
41	
42	
43	class ExtraConfigDefault(enum.Enum):
44	    """Centralized default values for extra_config keys.
45	
46	    Each member's *name* is the key used in the extra_config dict,
47	    and its *value* is the default.
48	    """
49	
50	    # Timeout (seconds) for blocking MQ requests: initial
51	    # chunk-size query, KV cache registration/unregistration,
52	    # and other synchronous operations.
53	    mq_timeout = 300.0
54	    # Interval (seconds) between periodic heartbeat pings
55	    # to the server.
56	    heartbeat_interval = 10.0
57	    # Routing mode for ``create_transfer_context``: ``auto`` keeps the
58	    # historical CUDA -> lmcache_driven / others -> engine_driven dispatch;
59	    # ``lmcache_driven`` forces the IPC / SHM zero-copy path where the
60	    # LMCache server pulls data via device handles;
61	    # ``engine_driven`` forces the worker-side gather/scatter copy path.
62	    # Mirrors the ``LMCACHE_MP_TRANSFER_MODE`` env var; this extra_config
63	    # key wins when both are set.
64	    mp_transfer_mode = "auto"
65	
66	
67	# Backward-compatible aliases for the legacy `lmcache_mp_connector_0180`
68	# entry point, which still passes these as positional/keyword args.
69	DEFAULT_MQ_TIMEOUT: float = ExtraConfigDefault.mq_timeout.value
70	DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL: float = ExtraConfigDefault.heartbeat_interval.value
71	
72	_EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX = "lmcache.mp."
73	
74	# Floor (seconds) of the MP server's worker reap timeout. It only covers the
75	# default 10 s heartbeat interval (3 x 10 s); the adapter warns at startup
76	# when 3 x heartbeat_interval exceeds it (server timeout must be raised too).
77	_SERVER_REAP_TIMEOUT_FLOOR_SECONDS: float = 30.0
78	
79	
80	def _resolve_extra_config(
81	    extra_config: dict[str, Any] | None,
82	) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    """Resolve extra_config against :class:`ExtraConfigDefault`.
84	
85	    Keys in *extra_config* are expected to carry the
86	    ``lmcache.mp.`` prefix (e.g. ``lmcache.mp.mq_timeout``).
87	    The prefix is stripped before matching against
88	    :class:`ExtraConfigDefault` members.
89	
90	    All ``lmcache.mp.*`` entries are logged.  Entries whose
91	    value differs from the default are additionally marked as
92	    *overridden*.
93	
94	    Args:
95	        extra_config: User-supplied config dict (may be *None*).
96	
97	    Returns:
98	        A dict keyed by :pyattr:`ExtraConfigDefault` member names.
99	    """
100	    stripped: dict[str, Any] = {}
101	    if extra_config is not None:
102	        for k, v in extra_config.items():
103	            if k.startswith(_EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX):
104	                short = k[len(_EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX) :]
105	                stripped[short] = v
106	
107	    resolved: dict[str, Any] = {}
108	    for item in ExtraConfigDefault:
109	        default = item.value
110	        raw = stripped.get(item.name)
111	        value = type(default)(raw) if raw is not None else default
112	        if value != default:
113	            logger.info(
114	                "%s%s = %s (overridden, default: %s)",
115	                _EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX,
116	                item.name,
117	                value,
118	                default,
119	            )
120	        else:
121	            logger.info(
122	                "%s%s = %s",
123	                _EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX,
124	                item.name,
125	                value,
126	            )
127	        resolved[item.name] = value
128	    return resolved

132	    def ipc_handle(self) -> Any: ...
133

137	def wrap_kv_caches(kv_caches: dict[str, torch.Tensor]) -> KVCache:
138	    # Emit a per-layer (name, shape, dtype) summary so the operator can
139	    # verify the exact layer set & tensor geometry being shipped to the
140	    # LMCache server, then the low-noise count of handles being wrapped.
141	    kept_summary = [
142	        (name, tuple(tensor.shape), str(tensor.dtype))
143	        for name, tensor in kv_caches.items()
144	    ]
145	    logger.debug(
146	        "KV cache transfer keeping %d layer(s) (name, shape, dtype):\n%s",
147	        len(kept_summary),
148	        "\n".join(
149	            f"  [{i}] {name}  shape={shape}  dtype={dtype}"
150	            for i, (name, shape, dtype) in enumerate(kept_summary)
151	        ),
152	    )
153	    logger.info("Wrapping %d KV cache tensors for IPC", len(kv_caches))
154	    # Per-iteration resource management: if wrapping the N-th tensor
155	    # raises, ``shm_unlink`` whatever earlier iterations already
156	    # registered with POSIX SHM so the named segments do not outlive
157	    # the failed batch. CUDA wrappers do not own a named segment and
158	    # are skipped via the duck-typed ``shm_name`` check.
159	    wrappers: KVCache = []
160	    try:
161	        for tensor in kv_caches.values():
162	            wrappers.append(wrap_one_kv_cache(tensor))
163	    except BaseException:
164	        _release_partial_kv_wrappers(wrappers)
165	        raise
166	    return wrappers
167	
168	
169	def _release_partial_kv_wrappers(wrappers: list[Any]) -> None:
170	    """Best-effort unlink of SHM segments owned by partially built wrappers.
171	
172	    Used by :func:`wrap_kv_caches` to roll back a half-finished batch
173	    when a later iteration raises. Only POSIX-SHM-backed wrappers carry
174	    a ``shm_name`` attribute, so other wrapper kinds (e.g. CUDA-IPC)
175	    are silently skipped.
176	    """
177	    # First Party
178	    from lmcache.v1.multiprocess.posix_shm import shm_unlink
179	
180	    for w in wrappers:
181	        name = getattr(w, "shm_name", None)
182	        if name is None:
183	            continue
184	        try:
185	            shm_unlink(name)
186	        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - best effort
187	            logger.debug("shm_unlink failed during rollback", exc_info=True)
188	
189	
190	def wrap_one_kv_cache(tensor: torch.Tensor) -> Any:
191	    """Dispatch by ``tensor.device.type`` via the platform registry.
192	
193	    Concrete factories are auto-discovered from
194	    ``DeviceIPCWrapper`` subclasses under ``lmcache.v1.platform``, so
195	    this call site stays free of if/elif chains and new accelerators
196	    plug in by shipping a sibling wrapper class.
197	    """
198	    return platform_registry.get_kv_wrapper_factory(tensor.device.type)(tensor)
199

201	def send_lmcache_request(
202	    mq_client: MessageQueueClient,
203	    request_type: RequestType,
204	    payloads: list[Any],
205	) -> MessagingFuture[Any]:
206	    """
207	    Helper function to send the request to the LMCache multiprocess server
208	
209	    Args:
210	        mq_client: The LMCache multiprocess mode message queue client
211	        request_type: The request type
212	        payloads: The request payloads
213	
214	    Returns:
215	        A messaging future for the request
216	    """
217	
218	    future = mq_client.submit_request(
219	        request_type, payloads, get_response_class(request_type)
220	    )
221	    return future

224	def get_lmcache_chunk_size(
225	    mq_client: MessageQueueClient,
226	    timeout: float = DEFAULT_MQ_TIMEOUT,
227	) -> int:
228	    """
229	    Helper function to get the LMCache chunk size from the server
230	
231	    Args:
232	        mq_client: The LMCache multiprocess mode message queue client
233	        timeout: Timeout in seconds for the blocking request.
234	
235	    Returns:
236	        An integer representing the LMCache chunk size
237	    """
238	    future = send_lmcache_request(mq_client, RequestType.GET_CHUNK_SIZE, [])
239	    lmcache_tokens_per_chunk = future.result(timeout=timeout)
240	    return lmcache_tokens_per_chunk

243	def _raise_server_unreachable(server_url: str, timeout: float) -> NoReturn:
244	    """Raise a verbose ConnectionError when the LMCache MP server is
245	    unreachable.
246	
247	    The message intentionally spells out the most common cause (the
248	    standalone ``lmcache server`` process is not running), the URL that
249	    was being dialed, and the exact command to start it -- so that users
250	    landing here via ``vllm serve --kv-offloading-backend lmcache`` are
251	    not left guessing.
252	    """
253	    hint = (
254	        "Cannot reach the LMCache MP server at "
255	        f"'{server_url}' within {timeout}s.\n"
256	        "This usually means the standalone LMCache server is not "
257	        "running, or it is listening on a different host/port.\n"
258	        "To start one locally with the default port (5555):\n"
259	        "    lmcache server --l1-size-gb 20 --eviction-policy LRU\n"
260	        "To target a different host/port, override via "
261	        "kv_connector_extra_config (lmcache.mp.host / lmcache.mp.port), "
262	        "e.g.:\n"
263	        '    --kv-transfer-config \'{"kv_connector":'
264	        '"LMCacheMPConnector","kv_role":"kv_both",'
265	        '"kv_connector_extra_config":{"lmcache.mp.host":'
266	        '"tcp://localhost","lmcache.mp.port":5555}}\'\n'
267	        "See https://docs.lmcache.ai/mp/quickstart.html for details."
268	    )
269	    logger.warning(hint)
270	    raise ConnectionError(hint) from None
271	
272	
273	def send_ping(
274	    mq_client: MessageQueueClient,
275	    timeout: float,
276	    instance_id: int | None = None,
277	) -> bool:
278	    """Send a PING request and return the result.
279	
280	    Args:
281	        mq_client: The message queue client.
282	        timeout: Seconds to wait for the server's response.
283	        instance_id: The worker's instance ID so the server can refresh its
284	            liveness, or None for an untracked prober (scheduler adapter).
285	
286	    Returns:
287	        True if server is healthy, False on timeout or error.
288	    """
289	    try:
290	        future = send_lmcache_request(mq_client, RequestType.PING, [instance_id])
291	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
292	    except TimeoutError:
293	        return False
294	    except Exception:
295	        logger.debug("Ping failed with exception", exc_info=True)
296	        return False

299	@dataclass
300	class ParallelStrategy:
301	    use_mla: bool
302	    """Whether to use the MLA."""
303	
304	    vllm_world_size: int
305	    """Number of workers managed by one vLLM scheduler (TP × PP; excludes DP).
306	
307	    Mirrors ``vllm.parallel_config.world_size``.
308	    """
309	
310	    vllm_worker_id: int
311	    """This worker's rank within its scheduler group."""
312	
313	    tp_size: int
314	    """The tensor parallel size."""
315	
316	    pp_size: int
317	    """The pipeline parallel size."""
318	
319	    n_servers: int
320	    """Number of LMCache servers backing this deployment"""
321	
322	    @property
323	    def kv_world_size(self) -> int:
324	        """Number of pieces a single token chunk's KV cache is split into
325	        on the LMCache server storage."""
326	        if self.use_mla:
327	            # In this PR we do not support PP + TP + MLA in multi-server mode.
328	            # A precondition check enforces pp_size == 1, so kv_world_size for
329	            # MLA can be derived as world_size / tp_size.
330	            return self.vllm_world_size // self.tp_size
331	        return self.vllm_world_size // self.n_servers
332	
333	    @property
334	    def kv_worker_id(self) -> int:
335	        """Index of the piece of a single token chunk's KV cache
336	        that the current worker is responsible for,
337	        in ``[0, kv_world_size)``."""
338	        if self.use_mla:
339	            return self.vllm_worker_id // self.tp_size
340	        return self.vllm_worker_id % (self.vllm_world_size // self.n_servers)
341	
342	    @property
343	    def kv_tp_size(self) -> int:
344	        """Tensor-parallel size as seen from a single LMCache server."""
345	        return self.tp_size // self.n_servers
346	
347	    @property
348	    def is_kv_writer(self) -> bool:
349	        """Whether this rank is responsible for storing KV."""
350	        if not self.use_mla:
351	            return True
352	        # MLA: only first rank per node is a writer.
353	        return self.vllm_worker_id % (self.tp_size // self.n_servers) == 0
354	
355	
356	def _normalize_adapter_init_args(
357	    vllm_block_size: int,
358	    parallel_strategy: ParallelStrategy | int,
359	    legacy_block_size: int | None,
360	    mq_timeout: float,
361	) -> tuple[int, ParallelStrategy, float]:
362	    """Normalize adapter constructor args from old and new vLLM connectors.
363	
364	    Args:
365	        vllm_block_size: The vLLM block size for the current connector API, or
366	            the legacy KV world size when ``parallel_strategy`` is an int.
367	        parallel_strategy: The current ``ParallelStrategy`` object, or the
368	            legacy KV worker id from older vLLM MP connectors.
369	        legacy_block_size: The legacy vLLM block size passed positionally by
370	            older vLLM MP connectors.
371	        mq_timeout: Timeout in seconds for synchronous message queue requests.
372	
373	    Returns:
374	        A tuple of normalized ``(vllm_block_size, parallel_strategy,
375	        mq_timeout)``.
376	
377	    Raises:
378	        TypeError: If the connector argument shape is not supported.
379	    """
380	    if isinstance(parallel_strategy, ParallelStrategy):
381	        return vllm_block_size, parallel_strategy, mq_timeout
382	    if not isinstance(parallel_strategy, int) or legacy_block_size is None:
383	        raise TypeError(
384	            "parallel_strategy must be ParallelStrategy, or legacy "
385	            "(kv_world_size, kv_worker_id, block_size) arguments"
386	        )
387	
388	    kv_world_size = int(vllm_block_size)
389	    kv_worker_id = int(parallel_strategy)
390	    strategy = ParallelStrategy(
391	        use_mla=False,
392	        vllm_world_size=kv_world_size,
393	        vllm_worker_id=kv_worker_id,
394	        tp_size=kv_world_size,
395	        pp_size=1,
396	        n_servers=1,
397	    )
398	    return int(legacy_block_size), strategy, mq_timeout

401	class HeartbeatThread(PeriodicThread):
402	    """Periodically checks server health via PING.
403	
404	    Manages a threading.Event that adapters use to gate operations.
405	    When unhealthy, the adapter enters degraded mode; if the server
406	    recovers, the adapter automatically resumes normal operation.
407	    """
408	
409	    def __init__(
410	        self,
411	        mq_client: MessageQueueClient,
412	        health_event: threading.Event,
413	        interval: float = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
414	        instance_id: int | None = None,
415	    ):
416	        """
417	        Args:
418	            mq_client: The message queue client used to send PING requests.
419	            health_event: A threading.Event shared with the adapter.
420	                Set when the server is healthy, cleared when unhealthy.
421	                Adapters check this event to decide whether to proceed
422	                with operations or enter degraded mode.
423	            interval: Seconds between heartbeat pings and ping timeout.
424	            instance_id: The worker's instance ID sent with each PING so the
425	                server can refresh its liveness, or None for an untracked
426	                prober (the scheduler adapter).
427	        """
428	        super().__init__(
429	            name="lmcache-heartbeat",
430	            interval=interval,
431	            level=ThreadLevel.CRITICAL,
432	        )
433	        self._mq_client = mq_client
434	        self._health_event = health_event
435	        self._interval = interval
436	        self._instance_id = instance_id
437	
438	        # Optional callback invoked on the unhealthy->healthy edge,
439	        # before the health event is set. See register_recover_callback.
440	        def noop() -> bool:
441	            return True
442	
443	        self._recover_callback: Callable[[], bool] = noop
444	
445	    def register_recover_callback(self, callback: Callable[[], bool]) -> None:
446	        """Register a callback fired on the unhealthy->healthy transition.
447	
448	        The callback runs **before** the health event is set. It must
449	        return ``True`` on success (event will be set) or ``False`` on
450	        failure (event will stay cleared, and the next heartbeat will
451	        invoke the callback again on the next successful PING).
452	
453	        The callback function should NEVER raise exceptions.
454	
455	        Intended for setup work that must complete before downstream
456	        callers observe the recovery — for example, re-registering KV
457	        caches with a server that just restarted.
458	
459	        Should be called before :meth:`start`. Only one callback is
460	        supported; a second call replaces the first.
461	
462	        Args:
463	            callback: Zero-arg callable returning a success bool.
464	        """
465	        self._recover_callback = callback
466	
467	    def _execute(self) -> ThreadRunSummary:
468	        """Run one heartbeat cycle: ping, recover callback, event update.
469	
470	        A cycle that observes a stop request returns without firing the
471	        callback or touching the event — a straggler success after
472	        UNREGISTER must not re-register a ghost context.
473	        """
474	        was_healthy = self._health_event.is_set()
475	        healthy = send_ping(
476	            self._mq_client, timeout=self._interval, instance_id=self._instance_id
477	        )
478	
479	        if self.stop_requested:
480	            return ThreadRunSummary(
481	                success=True,
482	                message="stop requested; skipping health update",
483	            )
484	
485	        need_trigger_recover = (
486	            healthy and not was_healthy and self._recover_callback is not None
487	        )
488	
489	        # Try to call recover callback
490	        if need_trigger_recover:
491	            logger.warning(
492	                "LMCache server is healthy again, triggering recovery callback"
493	            )
494	            # If the callback fails, it should not become healthy
495	            healthy = self._recover_callback()
496	
497	        if healthy:
498	            self._health_event.set()
499	            if not was_healthy:
500	                logger.warning(
501	                    "LMCache server is healthy again — resuming normal operation"
502	                )
503	        else:
504	            self._health_event.clear()
505	            if was_healthy:
506	                logger.warning("LMCache server is unhealthy — entering degraded mode")
507	
508	        return ThreadRunSummary(
509	            success=True,
510	            message="healthy" if healthy else "unhealthy",
511	        )
512	
513	
514	@dataclass
515	class LoadStoreOp:
516	    token_ids: list[int]
517	    """Token IDs for the load/store operation"""
518	
519	    block_ids: list[list[int]]
520	    """Block IDs for the load/store operation, indexed by engine KV cache
521	    group (one inner list per engine group). Worker submit paths expand
522	    this to LMCache KV group order before sending requests to the server.
523	    """
524	
525	    start: int = 0
526	    """Start token index"""
527	
528	    end: int = 0
529	    """End token index"""
530	
531	    skip_first_n_tokens: int = 0
532	    """Number of tokens to skip writing at the beginning of the retrieve
533	    range. Used to avoid overwriting APC-shared GPU blocks during retrieve."""
534	
535	    @property
536	    def flat_block_ids(self) -> list[int]:
537	        """Return all block IDs flattened for group-blind error paths.
538	
539	        Handles both the normal ``list[list[int]]`` format and the
540	        IPC-flattened ``list[int]`` format that vLLM v0.19.0 produces when
541	        ``SchedulerOutput`` serializes single-element nested lists across
542	        process boundaries (e.g. ``[[20, 21]]`` → ``[20, 21]``).
543	        Returns an empty list when ``block_ids`` is empty.
544	        """
545	        if not self.block_ids:
546	            return []
547	        # Defend against IPC serialization flattening [[20, 21, …]] → [20, 21, …]
548	        if isinstance(self.block_ids[0], int):
549	            return list(self.block_ids)
550	        return [
551	            block_id
552	            for group_block_ids in self.block_ids
553	            for block_id in group_block_ids
554	        ]

562	class LMCacheMPSchedulerAdapter:
563	    def __init__(
564	        self,
565	        server_urls: list[str],
566	        context: zmq.Context,
567	        model_name: str,
568	        vllm_block_size: int,
569	        parallel_strategy: ParallelStrategy | int,
570	        legacy_block_size: int | None = None,
571	        *,
572	        mq_timeout: float = DEFAULT_MQ_TIMEOUT,
573	        heartbeat_interval: float = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
574	        extra_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
575	    ):
576	        """
577	        Args:
578	            server_urls: The servers URL for the LMCache message queue
579	            context: The ZMQ context
580	            model_name: The model name used for LMCache keys
581	            vllm_block_size: The block size used in vLLM
582	            parallel_strategy:
583	                The parallel strategy, which includes `use_mla`,
584	                `kv_world_size`, `kv_worker_id` and so on. Older vLLM
585	                connectors pass the KV worker id here.
586	            legacy_block_size: The vLLM block size passed positionally by
587	                older vLLM connectors.
588	            mq_timeout: Timeout in seconds for message queue requests.
589	                Ignored when ``extra_config`` is provided.
590	            heartbeat_interval: Interval in seconds between heartbeat pings.
591	                Ignored when ``extra_config`` is provided.
592	            extra_config: Optional dict with keys starting with
593	                ``lmcache.mp.`` (e.g., ``lmcache.mp.mq_timeout``). When
594	                provided, it overrides ``mq_timeout`` / ``heartbeat_interval``.
595	        """
596	        vllm_block_size, parallel_strategy, mq_timeout = _normalize_adapter_init_args(
597	            vllm_block_size,
598	            parallel_strategy,
599	            legacy_block_size,
600	            mq_timeout,
601	        )
602	        assert len(server_urls) >= 1, "At least one server url required"
603	        self._server_urls: list[str] = list(server_urls)
604	        self.mq_clients: dict[str, MessageQueueClient] = {
605	            url: MessageQueueClient(url, context) for url in self._server_urls
606	        }
607	        if extra_config is not None:
608	            cfg = _resolve_extra_config(extra_config)
609	            mq_timeout = cfg[ExtraConfigDefault.mq_timeout.name]
610	            heartbeat_interval = cfg[ExtraConfigDefault.heartbeat_interval.name]
611	        self._mq_timeout = mq_timeout
612	
613	        # Lookup state tracking:
614	        # - _pending_lookups: request_ids submitted but not yet resolved
615	        # - _finished_lookup_results: cached chunk count keyed by request_id,
616	        #   so that repeated calls to check_lookup_result return the same value
617	        #   even after the server has already popped the job (exactly-once).
618	        # - _per_server_hits: {request_id: {server_url: hit_chunks}}.
619	        #   Per-server hit counts, used to detect disagreement and free tail locks.
620	        self._pending_lookups: set[str] = set()
621	        self._finished_lookup_results: dict[str, int] = {}
622	        self._per_server_hits: dict[str, dict[str, int]] = {}
623	        self._lookup_params: dict[str, tuple[list[int], str]] = {}
624	
625	        self.model_name = model_name
626	        self.parallel_strategy = parallel_strategy
627	
628	        # Read chunk size from lmcache
629	        chunk_sizes: dict[str, int] = {}
630	        for url, client in self.mq_clients.items():
631	            try:
632	                chunk_sizes[url] = get_lmcache_chunk_size(
633	                    client, timeout=self._mq_timeout
634	                )
635	            except TimeoutError:
636	                for c in self.mq_clients.values():
637	                    c.close()
638	                _raise_server_unreachable(url, self._mq_timeout)
639	
640	        # All servers must share chunk_size, otherwise the min() aggregation
641	        # over per-server hits would mix different granularities.
642	        unique_sizes = set(chunk_sizes.values())
643	        if len(unique_sizes) != 1:
644	            raise ValueError(
645	                f"All LMCache servers must share the same chunk_size, got {chunk_sizes}"
646	            )
647	        self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk = unique_sizes.pop()
648	
649	        assert self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk % vllm_block_size == 0, (
650	            "LMCache chunk size should be a multiple of vLLM block size"
651	        )
652	        self.blocks_in_chunk = self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk // vllm_block_size
653	
654	        # Health state: one Event per server. The adapter is considered healthy
655	        # only if ALL per-server events are set (any unhealthy server taints
656	        # the whole adapter, matching the min() semantics used for lookups).
657	        self._health_events: dict[str, threading.Event] = {}
658	        for url in self._server_urls:
659	            ev = threading.Event()
660	            ev.set()
661	            self._health_events[url] = ev
662	
663	        # Heartbeat thread is created but NOT started yet.
664	        # It will be lazily started on the first lookup
665	        # request, by which time vLLM is fully ready.
666	        self._heartbeat_interval = heartbeat_interval
667	        self._heartbeats: dict[str, HeartbeatThread] = {}
668	        self._heartbeat_lock = threading.Lock()
669	
670	    @property
671	    def world_size(self) -> int:
672	        """Get the kv world size."""
673	        return self.parallel_strategy.kv_world_size
674	
675	    @property
676	    def tp_size(self) -> int:
677	        """The tensor parallel size."""
678	        return self.parallel_strategy.kv_tp_size
679	
680	    @property
681	    def is_healthy(self) -> bool:
682	        """True iff every backing LMCache server is healthy."""
683	        return all(ev.is_set() for ev in self._health_events.values())
684	
685	    def _ensure_heartbeat_started(self) -> None:
686	        """Lazily start the heartbeat thread on first use."""
687	        if self._heartbeats is not None:
688	            return
689	        with self._heartbeat_lock:
690	            if self._heartbeats is not None:
691	                return
692	            for url, client in self.mq_clients.items():
693	                hb = HeartbeatThread(
694	                    mq_client=client,
695	                    health_event=self._health_events[url],
696	                    interval=self._heartbeat_interval,
697	                )
698	                hb.start()
699	                self._heartbeats[url] = hb
700	
701	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
702	    def maybe_submit_lookup_request(
703	        self,
704	        request_id: str,
705	        token_ids: list[int],
706	        cache_salt: str = "",
707	    ):
708	        """
709	        Submit a new lookup request to LMCache if there is no ongoing request.
710	
711	        Sends a LOOKUP request to the server and blocks until a prefetch
712	        job ID is returned.  The actual prefetch result can then be polled
713	        via ``check_lookup_result``.
714	
715	        Args:
716	            request_id: The ID of the lookup request. The same ID indicates it's
717	                from the same request
718	            token_ids: Token IDs to lookup from LMCache
719	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt. Requests with different
720	                cache_salt values produce separate cache entries.
721	
722	        Returns:
723	            None
724	
725	        Notes:
726	            This function will have a side-effect: submitting a look up request to
727	            LMCache, which will essentially 'lock' the KV cache chunks in the LMCache
728	            for later retrieve operations.
729	            In the meantime, this function will record the lookup request, and the
730	            status of the look up request can be checked by `check_lookup_result`.
731	        """
732	        self._ensure_heartbeat_started()
733	
734	        if not self.is_healthy:
735	            return
736	
737	        if request_id in self._pending_lookups:
738	            # Skip if there is already a lookup request
739	            return
740	
741	        aligned_end = (
742	            len(token_ids) // self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk
743	        ) * self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk
744	
745	        key = self._create_key(
746	            token_ids,
747	            start=0,
748	            end=aligned_end,
749	            request_id=request_id,
750	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
751	        ).no_worker_id_version()
752	
753	        futures: dict[str, MessagingFuture[Any]] = {
754	            url: send_lmcache_request(
755	                self.mq_clients[url],
756	                RequestType.LOOKUP,
757	                [key, self.tp_size],
758	            )
759	            for url in self._server_urls
760	        }
761	
762	        # Any one server failure means the whole lookup fails.
763	        for url, fut in futures.items():
764	            try:
765	                fut.result(timeout=self._mq_timeout)
766	            except TimeoutError:
767	                logger.warning(
768	                    "LOOKUP to %s timed out after %ss. Marking server as unhealthy.",
769	                    url,
770	                    self._mq_timeout,
771	                )
772	                self._health_events[url].clear()
773	                return
774	
775	        self._pending_lookups.add(request_id)
776	        self._lookup_params[request_id] = (token_ids, cache_salt)
777	
778	    def _free_inconsistent_lookup_locks(
779	        self,
780	        request_id: str,
781	        per_server: dict[str, int],
782	        min_chunks: int,
783	    ) -> None:
784	        """Release over-hit tail locks on servers that reported more than min.
785	
786	        When servers disagree on hit chunk counts, servers reporting more
787	        than min_chunks have locked chunks in the range
788	        [min_chunks * tokens_per_chunk, hit_chunks * tokens_per_chunk)
789	        that will never be retrieved. This method frees those tail locks.
790	
791	        Args:
792	            request_id: The lookup request ID.
793	            per_server: Per-server hit chunk counts.
794	            min_chunks: Minimum hit chunk count across all servers.
795	        """
796	        token_ids_l, cs = self._lookup_params.pop(request_id, (None, None))
797	        if token_ids_l is not None:
798	            for url, hit_chunks in per_server.items():
799	                if hit_chunks <= min_chunks:
800	                    continue
801	                tail_end = min(
802	                    hit_chunks * self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk, len(token_ids_l)
803	                )
804	                tail_key = self._create_key(
805	                    token_ids=token_ids_l,
806	                    start=min_chunks * self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk,
807	                    end=tail_end,
808	                    request_id=request_id,
809	                    cache_salt=cs or "",
810	                ).no_worker_id_version()
811	                send_lmcache_request(
812	                    self.mq_clients[url],
813	                    RequestType.FREE_LOOKUP_LOCKS,
814	                    [tail_key, self.tp_size],
815	                )
816	
817	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
818	    def check_lookup_result(self, request_id: str) -> int | None:
819	        """
820	        Check the result of a previously submitted lookup request.
821	
822	        Sends a QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS request to the servers and blocks
823	        until the server responds.  Returns the matched token count
824	        when the prefetch is complete, or None if still in progress.
825	
826	        Args:
827	            request_id: The ID of the lookup request submitted in
828	                `maybe_submit_lookup_request`
829	
830	        Returns:
831	            An integer representing the total number of tokens matched
832	            in LMCache (prefix matching), or
833	            None if the lookup request is not finished yet.
834	        """
835	        if request_id not in self._pending_lookups:
836	            # No job — either unhealthy at submit time or already cleaned up.
837	            # Return the cached aggregate if any, otherwise 0.
838	            return self._finished_lookup_results.get(request_id, 0)
839	
840	        if not self.is_healthy:
841	            # Server went down — give up on this lookup
842	            return 0
843	
844	        if request_id in self._finished_lookup_results:
845	            # Aggregation already done; return the cached value.
846	            return self._finished_lookup_results[request_id]
847	
848	        # Persistent accumulator for this request. A server present in
849	        # the dict has already handed over its final hit count and must
850	        # not be polled again; absence means "not yet observed".
851	        per_server = self._per_server_hits.setdefault(request_id, {})
852	        unresolved_urls = [u for u in self._server_urls if u not in per_server]
853	
854	        futures: dict[str, MessagingFuture[Any]] = {
855	            url: send_lmcache_request(
856	                self.mq_clients[url],
857	                RequestType.QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS,
858	                [request_id],
859	            )
860	            for url in unresolved_urls
861	        }
862	
863	        for url, fut in futures.items():
864	            try:
865	                r = fut.result(timeout=self._mq_timeout)
866	            except TimeoutError:
867	                logger.warning(
868	                    "QUERY_PREFETCH_STATUS to %s timed out. Marking unhealthy.",
869	                    url,
870	                )
871	                self._health_events[url].clear()
872	                return 0
873	            if r is None:
874	                continue
875	            per_server[url] = int(r)
876	
877	        if len(per_server) < len(self._server_urls):
878	            return None
879	
880	        min_chunks = min(per_server.values())
881	        max_chunks = max(per_server.values())
882	        if min_chunks != max_chunks:
883	            logger.warning(
884	                "[req=%s] LMCache hit mismatch across servers: %s → take min=%d",
885	                request_id,
886	                dict(per_server),
887	                min_chunks,
888	            )
889	            self._free_inconsistent_lookup_locks(request_id, per_server, min_chunks)
890	
891	        token_count = min_chunks * self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk
892	
893	        self._finished_lookup_results[request_id] = token_count
894	        return token_count
895	
896	    def num_blocks_per_chunk(self) -> int:
897	        """
898	        Returns:
899	            The number of vllm blocks in a LMCache data chunk
900	        """
901	        return self.blocks_in_chunk
902	
903	    def cleanup_lookup_result(self, request_id: str) -> None:
904	        """
905	        Clean up lookup state for a finished request to prevent memory leak.
906	        Args:
907	            request_id: The ID of the finished request.
908	        """
909	        self._pending_lookups.discard(request_id)
910	        self._finished_lookup_results.pop(request_id, None)
911	        self._per_server_hits.pop(request_id, None)
912	        self._lookup_params.pop(request_id, None)
913	
914	    def shutdown(self) -> None:
915	        """Shutdown the scheduler adapter and its resources."""
916	        for client in self.mq_clients.values():
917	            client.close()
918	        with self._heartbeat_lock:
919	            for hb in self._heartbeats.values():
920	                hb.stop()
921	
922	    def free_lookup_locks(
923	        self,
924	        token_ids: list[int],
925	        start: int,
926	        end: int,
927	        request_id: str,
928	        cache_salt: str = "",
929	    ) -> None:
930	        """Release read locks acquired during lookup without a full retrieve.
931	
932	        Use this when some chunks matched by lookup overlap with blocks that
933	        vLLM has already computed, so they will never be retrieved.  Calling
934	        this prevents those chunks from holding read locks until TTL expiry.
935	
936	        Or use this when a request is cancelled or aborted after lookup but
937	        before retrieve to avoid holding read locks until TTL expiry.
938	
939	        When ``start`` or ``end`` is not aligned to the chunk size, the
940	        entire chunk containing start boundary is freed but not end boundary.
941	        It is caller's responsibility to properly align the boundaries.
942	
943	        Args:
944	            token_ids: Token IDs for the key (same as used in lookup).
945	            start: Start token index.
946	            end: End token index.
947	            request_id: The request ID.
948	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt.
949	        """
950	        if not self.is_healthy:
951	            return
952	
953	        # Free [start, end) on every server.
954	        base_key = self._create_key(
955	            token_ids,
956	            start=start,
957	            end=end,
958	            request_id=request_id,
959	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
960	        ).no_worker_id_version()
961	        for url in self._server_urls:
962	            send_lmcache_request(
963	                self.mq_clients[url],
964	                RequestType.FREE_LOOKUP_LOCKS,
965	                [base_key, self.tp_size],
966	            )
967	
968	    def end_session(self, request_id: str) -> None:
969	        """
970	        Notify LMCache server to remove the session for a finished request.
971	        Args:
972	            request_id: The ID of the finished request.
973	        """
974	        if not self.is_healthy:
975	            return
976	
977	        for url in self._server_urls:
978	            send_lmcache_request(
979	                self.mq_clients[url],
980	                RequestType.END_SESSION,
981	                [request_id],
982	            )
983	
984	    def report_block_allocations(
985	        self,
986	        records: list[BlockAllocationRecord],
987	    ) -> None:
988	        """Report vLLM GPU block allocation deltas to LMCache server.
989	
990	        Fire-and-forget: does not wait for a response. If the server
991	        is unhealthy the report is silently dropped.
992	
993	        Args:
994	            records: List of BlockAllocationRecord with per-request
995	                block and token allocation deltas.
996	        """
997	        if not self.is_healthy or not records:
998	            return
999	
1000	        for url in self._server_urls:
1001	            send_lmcache_request(
1002	                self.mq_clients[url],
1003	                RequestType.REPORT_BLOCK_ALLOCATION,
1004	                [os.getpid(), self.model_name, records],
1005	            )
1006	
1007	    # Helper functions
1008	    def _create_key(
1009	        self,
1010	        token_ids: list[int],
1011	        start: int,
1012	        end: int,
1013	        request_id: str,
1014	        cache_salt: str = "",
1015	    ) -> IPCCacheServerKey:
1016	        """Convert token IDs to an IPC cache engine key.
1017	
1018	        Args:
1019	            token_ids: The token IDs.
1020	            start: Start token index.
1021	            end: End token index.
1022	            request_id: The request ID.
1023	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt.
1024	
1025	        Returns:
1026	            IPCCacheServerKey: The constructed key.
1027	        """
1028	        # NOTE: for the scheduler adapter, we don't have a worker id,
1029	        # so we set it to None in the key.
1030	        return IPCCacheServerKey(
1031	            model_name=self.model_name,
1032	            world_size=self.world_size,
1033	            worker_id=None,
1034	            token_ids=tuple(token_ids),
1035	            start=start,
1036	            end=end,
1037	            request_id=request_id,
1038	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
1039	        )

1042	class LMCacheMPWorkerAdapter:
1043	    def __init__(
1044	        self,
1045	        server_url: str,
1046	        context: zmq.Context,
1047	        model_name: str,
1048	        vllm_block_size: int,
1049	        parallel_strategy: ParallelStrategy | int,
1050	        legacy_block_size: int | None = None,
1051	        *,
1052	        mq_timeout: float = DEFAULT_MQ_TIMEOUT,
1053	        heartbeat_interval: float = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
1054	        extra_config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
1055	    ):
1056	        """Initialize the worker adapter for current or legacy vLLM callers.
1057	
1058	        Args:
1059	            server_url: The server URL for the LMCache message queue.
1060	            context: The ZMQ context.
1061	            model_name: The model name used for LMCache keys.
1062	            vllm_block_size: The block size used in vLLM, or legacy KV world
1063	                size when ``parallel_strategy`` is an int.
1064	            parallel_strategy: Current ``ParallelStrategy`` metadata, or the
1065	                legacy KV worker id from older vLLM connectors.
1066	            legacy_block_size: The vLLM block size passed positionally by
1067	                older vLLM connectors.
1068	            mq_timeout: Timeout in seconds for message queue requests.
1069	                Ignored when ``extra_config`` is provided.
1070	            heartbeat_interval: Interval in seconds between heartbeat pings.
1071	                Ignored when ``extra_config`` is provided.
1072	            extra_config: Optional dict with keys starting with
1073	                ``lmcache.mp.`` (e.g., ``lmcache.mp.mq_timeout``). When
1074	                provided, it overrides ``mq_timeout`` / ``heartbeat_interval``.
1075	
1076	        Raises:
1077	            TypeError: If the connector argument shape is unsupported.
1078	        """
1079	        vllm_block_size, parallel_strategy, mq_timeout = _normalize_adapter_init_args(
1080	            vllm_block_size,
1081	            parallel_strategy,
1082	            legacy_block_size,
1083	            mq_timeout,
1084	        )
1085	        if extra_config is not None:
1086	            cfg = _resolve_extra_config(extra_config)
1087	            mq_timeout = cfg[ExtraConfigDefault.mq_timeout.name]
1088	            heartbeat_interval = cfg[ExtraConfigDefault.heartbeat_interval.name]
1089	            # Only treat ``mp_transfer_mode`` as an explicit override when
1090	            # the user actually set it in extra_config; otherwise leave it
1091	            # as ``None`` so ``create_transfer_context`` can still consult
1092	            # the ``LMCACHE_MP_TRANSFER_MODE`` env var.
1093	            mp_mode_key = (
1094	                _EXTRA_CONFIG_KEY_PREFIX + ExtraConfigDefault.mp_transfer_mode.name
1095	            )
1096	            if mp_mode_key in extra_config:
1097	                self._mp_transfer_mode = cfg[ExtraConfigDefault.mp_transfer_mode.name]
1098	            else:
1099	                self._mp_transfer_mode = None
1100	        else:
1101	            self._mp_transfer_mode = None
1102	        self.mq_client = MessageQueueClient(server_url, context)
1103	        self._mq_timeout = mq_timeout
1104	
1105	        # Instance id for GPU worker. uuid4-derived (OS entropy) rather
1106	        # than os.getpid() to avoid collision in containerized deployments.
1107	        # Masked to 63 bits to stay signed-int64-safe for any msgpack peer.
1108	        self.instance_id: int = uuid.uuid4().int & ((1 << 63) - 1)
1109	        logger.info(
1110	            "LMCache MP worker adapter created with instance_id=%d",
1111	            self.instance_id,
1112	        )
1113	
1114	        # Registered kv caches from vLLM
1115	        self.kv_caches: dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
1116	        self.engine_group_infos: list[EngineGroupInfo] = []
1117	
1118	        # Transport context for transfer operations.
1119	        self.transfer_ctx: TransferContext | None = None
1120	
1121	        # Request futures
1122	        self.store_futures: dict[str, MessagingFuture[StoreResult]] = {}
1123	        # request_id -> (future, block_ids)
1124	        self.retrieve_futures: dict[
1125	            str, tuple[MessagingFuture[RetrieveResult], list[int]]
1126	        ] = {}
1127	        # The IPC handle is not enough by itself; CUDA needs the exporting
1128	        # event object to stay alive until the consumer is done with it.
1129	        self.store_events: dict[str, _IpcEvent] = {}
1130	        self.retrieve_events: dict[str, _IpcEvent] = {}
1131	
1132	        # Block IDs that failed due to retrieve timeout
1133	        self.error_block_ids: set[int] = set()
1134	
1135	        # Retrieve request ids dropped by the unhealthy early-return of
1136	        # submit_retrieve_request. get_finished must still report each id
1137	        # exactly once, or async loads hang in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS.
1138	        self._dropped_retrieves: set[str] = set()
1139	
1140	        # The store requests that have finished execution in LMCache
1141	        self.finished_stores: set[str] = set()
1142	        # The finished request ids that are passed via vLLM and also
1143	        # have corresponding store requests submitted to LMCache before
1144	        self.previously_finished: set[str] = set()
1145	        # Request IDs already returned as finished_sending to the scheduler.
1146	        # Prevents re-reporting the same ID after drain clears tracking sets.
1147	        self._returned_finished: set[str] = set()
1148	
1149	        self.model_name = model_name
1150	        self.parallel_strategy = parallel_strategy
1151	
1152	        # Read chunk size from lmcache
1153	        try:
1154	            lmcache_tokens_per_chunk = get_lmcache_chunk_size(
1155	                self.mq_client, timeout=self._mq_timeout
1156	            )
1157	        except TimeoutError:
1158	            self.mq_client.close()
1159	            _raise_server_unreachable(server_url, self._mq_timeout)
1160	        self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk = lmcache_tokens_per_chunk
1161	        assert lmcache_tokens_per_chunk % vllm_block_size == 0, (
1162	            "LMCache chunk size should be a multiple of vLLM block size"
1163	        )
1164	        self.blocks_in_chunk = lmcache_tokens_per_chunk // vllm_block_size
1165	
1166	        # Health state (shared with heartbeat thread)
1167	        self._health_event = threading.Event()
1168	        self._health_event.set()
1169	
1170	        # Heartbeat thread is created but NOT started yet.
1171	        # It will be lazily started on the first store or retrieve
1172	        # request, by which time vLLM is fully ready (model loaded,
1173	        # KV caches allocated, warmup & CUDA graph capture done).
1174	        self._heartbeat_interval = heartbeat_interval
1175	        self._heartbeat: HeartbeatThread | None = None
1176	        self._heartbeat_lock = threading.Lock()
1177	        if 3 * heartbeat_interval > _SERVER_REAP_TIMEOUT_FLOOR_SECONDS:
1178	            logger.warning(
1179	                "lmcache.mp.heartbeat_interval is %.1fs, so 3 x "
1180	                "heartbeat_interval (%.1fs) exceeds the MP server's "
1181	                "default worker reap timeout floor (%.1fs). Raise the "
1182	                "server's worker reap timeout to at least 3 x the "
1183	                "heartbeat interval, or the server may reap this "
1184	                "worker between heartbeats.",
1185	                heartbeat_interval,
1186	                3 * heartbeat_interval,
1187	                _SERVER_REAP_TIMEOUT_FLOOR_SECONDS,
1188	            )
1189	
1190	        # request telemetry, used for prefill-decode disagg
1191	        # TODO: pass down the configuration via vLLM connector config
1192	        # instead of env var
1193	        self.request_telemetry = RequestTelemetryFactory.create(
1194	            telemetry_type=os.getenv("LMCACHE_REQUEST_TELEMETRY_TYPE", "noop"),
1195	            config={
1196	                "endpoint": os.getenv(
1197	                    "LMCACHE_REQUEST_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT",
1198	                    "http://localhost:5768/api/v1/telemetry",
1199	                ),
1200	            },
1201	        )
1202

1207	        Reflects the most recent heartbeat result. KV cache
1208	        re-registration on the unhealthy->healthy transition is handled
1209	        by the heartbeat thread itself via ``register_recover_callback``,
1210	        so this property only reads the shared event.
1211	        """
1212	        return self._health_event.is_set()

1229	    def register_kv_caches(
1230	        self,
1231	        kv_caches: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
1232	        engine_group_infos: Sequence[EngineGroupInfo] = (),
1233	    ) -> None:
1234	        """
1235	        Register the kv caches with LMCache server.
1236	
1237	        Args:
1238	            kv_caches: A dict of kv caches to register. The keys are the
1239	                layer names and the values are the corresponding tensors.
1240	            engine_group_infos: LMCache-owned engine KV cache group metadata.
1241	
1242	        Raises:
1243	            ConnectionError: if the server does not respond within
1244	                mq_timeout.
1245	            ValueError: if the LMCache chunk size is not a multiple of an
1246	                engine group's ``tokens_per_block`` (chunk boundaries would
1247	                not align with that group's paged-chunk boundaries).
1248	        """
1249	        logger.info("Registering kv caches")
1250	        for info in engine_group_infos:
1251	            if (
1252	                info.tokens_per_block > 0
1253	                and self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk % info.tokens_per_block
1254	            ):
1255	                raise ValueError(
1256	                    f"LMCache chunk size {self.lmcache_tokens_per_chunk} must be a "
1257	                    f"multiple of engine group {info.engine_group_id} "
1258	                    f"tokens_per_block {info.tokens_per_block}"
1259	                )
1260	        self.kv_caches = kv_caches
1261	        self.engine_group_infos = list(engine_group_infos)
1262	        self._send_register_kv_caches_request(kv_caches)
1263	
1264	    def _block_ids_per_group(self, op: LoadStoreOp) -> list[list[int]]:
1265	        return expand_engine_block_ids(self.engine_group_infos, op.block_ids)
1266	
1267	    def _send_register_kv_caches_request(
1268	        self,
1269	        kv_caches: dict[str, torch.Tensor],
1270	    ) -> None:
1271	        """Submit a REGISTER_KV_CACHE request and wait for the response.
1272	
1273	        Shared by the public ``register_kv_caches`` entry point and the
1274	        heartbeat recovery path (``_reregister_kv_caches_callback``).
1275	
1276	        Args:
1277	            kv_caches: The KV cache dict to register.
1278	
1279	        Raises:
1280	            ConnectionError: if the server does not respond within
1281	                mq_timeout.
1282	        """
1283	        self.kv_caches = kv_caches
1284	        transfer_ctx = create_transfer_context(kv_caches, mode=self._mp_transfer_mode)
1285	        layout_hints = vllm_layout_hints()
1286	        self.transfer_ctx = transfer_ctx
1287	        try:
1288	            # Register on the local, not self.transfer_ctx: a concurrent
1289	            # shutdown() may null self.transfer_ctx between publish and this
1290	            # call. The local is always non-None.
1291	            transfer_ctx.register(
1292	                self.instance_id,
1293	                kv_caches,
1294	                self.model_name,
1295	                self.world_size,
1296	                self.blocks_in_chunk,
1297	                self.mq_client,
1298	                self._mq_timeout,
1299	                send_request=send_lmcache_request,
1300	                layout_hints=layout_hints,
1301	                engine_group_infos=self.engine_group_infos,
1302	            )
1303	        except TimeoutError:
1304	            raise ConnectionError(
1305	                "LMCache server did not respond to "
1306	                "register_kv_caches within "
1307	                f"{self._mq_timeout}s. Is the server running?"
1308	            ) from None
1309	
1310	    def _ensure_heartbeat_started(self) -> None:
1311	        """Lazily start the heartbeat thread on first store/retrieve.
1312	
1313	        The heartbeat starts healthy (the event was set at construction). A
1314	        live worker pings every interval, refreshing its server-side
1315	        ``last_seen``, so it is never reaped while alive -- no re-registration
1316	        is needed at startup, and the first store/retrieve is not gated. The
1317	        recover callback still re-registers on a genuine unhealthy->healthy
1318	        edge (server restart).
1319	        """
1320	        if self._heartbeat is not None:
1321	            return
1322	        with self._heartbeat_lock:
1323	            if self._heartbeat is not None:
1324	                return
1325	            heartbeat = HeartbeatThread(
1326	                mq_client=self.mq_client,
1327	                health_event=self._health_event,
1328	                interval=self._heartbeat_interval,
1329	                instance_id=self.instance_id,
1330	            )
1331	            heartbeat.register_recover_callback(self._reregister_kv_caches_callback)
1332	            heartbeat.start()
1333	            self._heartbeat = heartbeat
1334	
1335	    def _heartbeat_stop_requested(self) -> bool:
1336	        """Whether a created heartbeat thread has a stop requested.
1337	
1338	        Returns:
1339	            ``True`` only if a heartbeat exists and its stop was requested.
1340	        """
1341	        heartbeat = self._heartbeat
1342	        return heartbeat is not None and heartbeat.stop_requested
1343	
1344	    def _reregister_kv_caches_callback(self) -> bool:
1345	        """Heartbeat recover callback: re-register KV caches after the
1346	        server returns. Runs on the heartbeat thread, before the health
1347	        event is set.
1348	
1349	        Returns:
1350	            ``True`` if nothing needs re-registering or registration
1351	            succeeds; ``False`` on failure or a requested heartbeat stop
1352	            (event stays cleared; retried on the next successful PING).
1353	        """
1354	        if not self.kv_caches:
1355	            # Nothing was registered yet (server flapped before the
1356	            # very first register_kv_caches). Treat as success so the
1357	            # health event can be set.
1358	            return True
1359	
1360	        # Skip the rebuild if a shutdown already requested the heartbeat stop.
1361	        if self._heartbeat_stop_requested():
1362	            logger.info("Heartbeat stop requested; skipping KV cache re-registration")
1363	            return False
1364	
1365	        try:
1366	            self._send_register_kv_caches_request(self.kv_caches)
1367	        except ConnectionError:
1368	            logger.exception(
1369	                "Failed to re-register KV caches after server recovery; "
1370	                "will retry on next heartbeat"
1371	            )
1372	            return False
1373	        except Exception:
1374	            logger.exception(
1375	                "Unexpected error during KV cache re-registration; "
1376	                "will retry on next heartbeat"
1377	            )
1378	            return False
1379	        logger.warning("Finished re-registering KV caches after server recovery")
1380	        return True
1381	
1382	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1383	    def submit_store_request(
1384	        self,
1385	        request_id: str,
1386	        op: LoadStoreOp,
1387	        event: _IpcEvent,
1388	        cache_salt: str = "",
1389	    ):
1390	        """
1391	        Submit a KV cache store request to LMCache
1392	
1393	        Args:
1394	            request_id: The ID of the request
1395	            op: The LoadStoreOp describing the store operation.
1396	            event: The CUDA event that is recorded after the current
1397	                model inference step
1398	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt.
1399	        """
1400	        self._ensure_heartbeat_started()
1401	
1402	        if not self.is_kv_writer:
1403	            return
1404	
1405	        if not self.is_healthy:
1406	            return
1407	
1408	        assert op.token_ids is not None
1409	        key = self._create_key(
1410	            op.token_ids,
1411	            op.start,
1412	            op.end,
1413	            request_id=request_id,
1414	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
1415	        )
1416	        if self.transfer_ctx is None:
1417	            raise RuntimeError(
1418	                "Transfer context is not initialized. "
1419	                "Call register_kv_caches() before submitting store requests."
1420	            )
1421	        future = self.transfer_ctx.submit_store(
1422	            request_id,
1423	            key,
1424	            self.instance_id,
1425	            self.kv_caches,
1426	            self._block_ids_per_group(op),
1427	            event,
1428	            self.blocks_in_chunk,
1429	        )
1430	        self.store_futures[request_id] = future
1431	        self.store_events[request_id] = event
1432	
1433	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1434	    def submit_retrieve_request(
1435	        self,
1436	        request_id: str,
1437	        op: LoadStoreOp,
1438	        event: _IpcEvent,
1439	        cache_salt: str = "",
1440	    ) -> None:
1441	        """
1442	        Submit a KV cache retrieve request to LMCache
1443	
1444	        When the server is unhealthy the request is not submitted: blocks
1445	        are flagged via ``error_block_ids`` (vLLM recomputes) and the id is
1446	        recorded so ``get_finished`` still reports it exactly once.
1447	
1448	        Args:
1449	            request_id: The ID of the request
1450	            op: The LoadStoreOp describing the retrieve operation.
1451	            event: The CUDA event that is recorded after the current
1452	                model inference step
1453	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt.
1454	        """
1455	        self._ensure_heartbeat_started()
1456	
1457	        if not self.is_healthy:
1458	            self.error_block_ids.update(op.flat_block_ids)
1459	            self._dropped_retrieves.add(request_id)
1460	            return
1461	
1462	        assert op.token_ids is not None
1463	        key = self._create_key(
1464	            op.token_ids,
1465	            op.start,
1466	            op.end,
1467	            request_id=request_id,
1468	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
1469	        )
1470	        if self.transfer_ctx is None:
1471	            raise RuntimeError(
1472	                "Transfer context is not initialized. "
1473	                "Call register_kv_caches() before submitting retrieve requests."
1474	            )
1475	        future = self.transfer_ctx.submit_retrieve(
1476	            request_id,
1477	            key,
1478	            self.instance_id,
1479	            self.kv_caches,
1480	            self._block_ids_per_group(op),
1481	            event,
1482	            self.blocks_in_chunk,
1483	            skip_first_n_tokens=op.skip_first_n_tokens,
1484	        )
1485	        self.retrieve_futures[request_id] = (future, op.flat_block_ids)
1486	        self.retrieve_events[request_id] = event
1487	
1488	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1489	    def batched_submit_store_requests(
1490	        self,
1491	        request_ids: list[str],
1492	        ops: list[LoadStoreOp],
1493	        event: _IpcEvent,
1494	        cache_salts: list[str] | None = None,
1495	    ):
1496	        """
1497	        Submit a batched store request to LMCache
1498	
1499	        Args:
1500	            request_ids: The IDs of the requests
1501	            ops: The LoadStoreOps describing the store operations. Should have
1502	                the same length as request_ids
1503	            event: The CUDA event that is recorded after the current
1504	                model inference step
1505	            cache_salts: Per-user isolation salts, one per request. If None,
1506	                all requests use cache_salt="". The list length should be the same as
1507	                request_ids.
1508	        """
1509	        if cache_salts is None:
1510	            cache_salts = [""] * len(request_ids)
1511	        for request_id, op, salt in zip(request_ids, ops, cache_salts, strict=False):
1512	            self.submit_store_request(request_id, op, event, cache_salt=salt)
1513	
1514	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1515	    def batched_submit_retrieve_requests(
1516	        self,
1517	        request_ids: list[str],
1518	        ops: list[LoadStoreOp],
1519	        event: _IpcEvent,
1520	        cache_salts: list[str] | None = None,
1521	    ):
1522	        """
1523	        Submit a batched retrieve request to LMCache
1524	
1525	        Args:
1526	            request_ids: The IDs of the requests
1527	            ops: The LoadStoreOps describing the retrieve operations. Should have
1528	                the same length as request_ids
1529	            event: The CUDA event that is recorded after the current
1530	                model inference step
1531	            cache_salts: Per-user isolation salts, one per request. If None,
1532	                all requests use cache_salt="". The list length should be same as
1533	                request_ids.
1534	        """
1535	        if cache_salts is None:
1536	            cache_salts = [""] * len(request_ids)
1537	        for request_id, op, salt in zip(request_ids, ops, cache_salts, strict=False):
1538	            self.submit_retrieve_request(request_id, op, event, cache_salt=salt)
1539	
1540	    def _process_finished_stores(
1541	        self,
1542	        finished_req_ids_from_lmcache: set[str],
1543	        finished_req_ids_from_engine: set[str],
1544	    ) -> set[str]:
1545	        """Merge LMCache-side and engine-side finished store info."""
1546	        self.finished_stores.update(finished_req_ids_from_lmcache)
1547	        ret_stores = set()
1548	        for req_id in finished_req_ids_from_engine:
1549	            if req_id in self._returned_finished:
1550	                continue
1551	            if req_id in self.finished_stores or req_id in self.store_futures:
1552	                self.previously_finished.add(req_id)
1553	            else:
1554	                ret_stores.add(req_id)
1555	        ret_stores.update(self._update_and_get_finished_store())
1556	        self._returned_finished.update(ret_stores)
1557	        return ret_stores
1558	
1559	    @_lmcache_nvtx_annotate
1560	    def get_finished(
1561	        self, finished_req_ids_from_engine: set[str]
1562	    ) -> tuple[set[str] | None, set[str] | None]:
1563	        """
1564	        Check and get the finished store and retrieve requests.
1565	
1566	        Args:
1567	            finished_req_ids_from_engine: the set of request ids that are
1568	                reported as finished from the vLLM engine side.
1569	
1570	        Returns:
1571	            A tuple of two sets:
1572	            - The first set contains the finished store request ids. The returned
1573	                store request ids MUST be seen before in the
1574	                `finished_req_ids_from_engine`.
1575	            - The second set contains the finished retrieve request ids,
1576	                including retrieves dropped at submit time while unhealthy
1577	                (reported exactly once; blocks already in error_block_ids).
1578	
1579	        Notes:
1580	            When enabling async scheduling in vLLM, the same request ID may appear
1581	            multiple times in `finished_req_ids_from_engine`. The adapter should
1582	            take care of deduplicating the request IDs and only return the request
1583	            IDs that have not been returned before.
1584	        """
1585	        # If unhealthy, drain all pending futures immediately
1586	        if not self.is_healthy:
1587	            finished_stores = set(self.store_futures.keys())
1588	            finished_retrieves = set()
1589	            for request_id, (
1590	                _r_future,
1591	                r_block_ids,
1592	            ) in self.retrieve_futures.items():
1593	                finished_retrieves.add(request_id)
1594	                self.error_block_ids.update(r_block_ids)
1595	            self.store_futures.clear()
1596	            self.retrieve_futures.clear()
1597	            self.store_events.clear()
1598	            self.retrieve_events.clear()
1599	
1600	            # Retrieves dropped at submit time still must be reported,
1601	            # exactly once, or async loads hang in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS.
1602	            # Swap-drain (not update-then-clear): a concurrent
1603	            # submit_retrieve_request add lands in the old set (reported now)
1604	            # or the fresh set (reported next call), never lost.
1605	            dropped = self._dropped_retrieves
1606	            self._dropped_retrieves = set()
1607	            finished_retrieves.update(dropped)
1608	
1609	            ret_stores = self._process_finished_stores(
1610	                finished_stores, finished_req_ids_from_engine
1611	            )
1612	            # A request may have a pending retrieve AND appear in
1613	            # finished_req_ids_from_engine (it ran without loading KV after
1614	            # the server died).  The scheduler processes finished_recving
1615	            # first and deletes the request, so we must not also report it
1616	            # in finished_sending.
1617	            ret_stores -= finished_retrieves
1618	            return ret_stores, finished_retrieves
1619	
1620	        finished_stores = set()
1621	        finished_retrieves = set()
1622	        for request_id, s_future in self.store_futures.items():
1623	            if not s_future.query():
1624	                continue
1625	
1626	            s_result = s_future.result()
1627	            finished_stores.add(request_id)
1628	
1629	            if not s_result:
1630	                logger.error(
1631	                    "Something went wrong when processing the "
1632	                    "store request for request_id=%s",
1633	                    request_id,
1634	                )
1635	
1636	        for request_id, (r_future, _) in self.retrieve_futures.items():
1637	            if not r_future.query():
1638	                continue
1639	
1640	            r_result = r_future.result()
1641	            finished_retrieves.add(request_id)
1642	
1643	            if not r_result:
1644	                logger.error(
1645	                    "Something went wrong when processing the "
1646	                    "retrieve request for request_id=%s, result=%s",
1647	                    request_id,
1648	                    r_result,
1649	                )
1650	
1651	        # Remove the finished requests from the tracking dicts
1652	        for request_id in finished_stores:
1653	            self.store_futures.pop(request_id, None)
1654	            self.store_events.pop(request_id, None)
1655	        for request_id in finished_retrieves:
1656	            self.retrieve_futures.pop(request_id, None)
1657	            self.retrieve_events.pop(request_id, None)
1658	
1659	        # Retrieves dropped while unhealthy still must be reported,
1660	        # exactly once, or async loads hang in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS. No
1661	        # finished_sending dedup is needed (unlike the unhealthy branch): a
1662	        # dropped retrieve's request is parked in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS until
1663	        # this report, so it cannot also be engine-finished in the same call.
1664	        # Swap-drain so a concurrent submit_retrieve_request add is never lost.
1665	        dropped = self._dropped_retrieves
1666	        self._dropped_retrieves = set()
1667	        finished_retrieves.update(dropped)
1668	
1669	        # Update the internal states
1670	        ret_stores = self._process_finished_stores(
1671	            finished_stores, finished_req_ids_from_engine
1672	        )
1673	
1674	        # the invocation of `get_finished` means that
1675	        # these requests' KV caches are already fully stored.
1676	        # or the requests normally ends without any store.
1677	        if ret_stores:
1678	            self.request_telemetry.on_request_store_finished(
1679	                request_ids_set=ret_stores,
1680	                model_name=self.model_name,
1681	                world_size=self.world_size,
1682	                kv_rank=self.worker_id,
1683	            )
1684	
1685	        return ret_stores, finished_retrieves
1686	
1687	    def num_blocks_per_chunk(self) -> int:
1688	        """
1689	        Returns:
1690	            The number of vllm blocks in a LMCache data chunk
1691	        """
1692	        return self.blocks_in_chunk
1693	
1694	    def get_block_ids_with_load_errors(self) -> set[int]:
1695	        """
1696	        Returns the block IDs that failed due to retrieve timeout,
1697	        then clears the internal set.
1698	        """
1699	        errors = self.error_block_ids.copy()
1700	        self.error_block_ids.clear()
1701	        return errors
1702	
1703	    def handle_preemptions(self, need_flush_before_forward: bool) -> None:
1704	        """Handle worker-side preemption hints from connector metadata.
1705	
1706	        When ``need_flush_before_forward`` is true, synchronize deferred
1707	        engine-driven gather work before the next forward pass can overwrite
1708	        paged KV blocks.
1709	
1710	        Args:
1711	            need_flush_before_forward: When True, flush in-flight gather
1712	                operations on the transfer context. When False, this is a no-op.
1713	        """
1714	        if not need_flush_before_forward:
1715	            return
1716	        if not self.is_healthy or self.transfer_ctx is None:
1717	            return
1718	        self.transfer_ctx.flush_inflight_stores()
1719	        # Force device sync here, compare to preemption, perf panelty is trivial
1720	        torch_dev.synchronize()
1721	
1722	    def shutdown(self) -> None:
1723	        """
1724	        Shutdown the LMCache MP worker adapter.
1725	
1726	        Stops the heartbeat (if started) before UNREGISTER: no new ping
1727	        on the closing mq_client, and a straggler in-flight cycle cannot
1728	        re-register or flip the health event after unregistration.
1729	        """
1730	        with self._heartbeat_lock:
1731	            if self._heartbeat is not None:
1732	                self._heartbeat.stop()
1733	
1734	        logger.info("Unregistering kv caches")
1735	        try:
1736	            unregister_type = (
1737	                RequestType.UNREGISTER_KV_CACHE_ENGINE_DRIVEN_CONTEXT
1738	                if isinstance(self.transfer_ctx, EngineDrivenTransferContext)
1739	                else RequestType.UNREGISTER_KV_CACHE
1740	            )
1741	            send_lmcache_request(
1742	                self.mq_client,
1743	                unregister_type,
1744	                [self.instance_id],
1745	            ).result(timeout=self._mq_timeout)
1746	        except TimeoutError:
1747	            logger.warning(
1748	                "LMCache server did not respond to unregister within %ss. "
1749	                "Proceeding with shutdown.",
1750	                self._mq_timeout,
1751	            )
1752	
1753	        if self.transfer_ctx is not None:
1754	            self.transfer_ctx.close()
1755	            self.transfer_ctx = None
1756	
1757	        self.mq_client.close()
1758	        self.request_telemetry.close()
1759	
1760	    # Helper functions
1761	    def _update_and_get_finished_store(
1762	        self,
1763	    ) -> set[str]:
1764	        """Converge the internal states about finished stores
1765	        and returns the 'safe finished store request ids' back
1766	        """
1767	        safe_finished_s = self.finished_stores.intersection(self.previously_finished)
1768	        self.finished_stores.difference_update(self.previously_finished)
1769	        self.previously_finished.difference_update(safe_finished_s)
1770	
1771	        return safe_finished_s
1772	
1773	    def _create_key(
1774	        self,
1775	        token_ids: list[int],
1776	        start: int,
1777	        end: int,
1778	        request_id: str,
1779	        cache_salt: str = "",
1780	    ) -> IPCCacheServerKey:
1781	        """Convert token IDs to an IPC cache engine key.
1782	
1783	        Args:
1784	            token_ids: The token IDs.
1785	            start: Start token index.
1786	            end: End token index.
1787	            request_id: The request ID.
1788	            cache_salt: Per-user isolation salt.
1789	
1790	        Returns:
1791	            IPCCacheServerKey: The constructed key.
1792	        """
1793	        return IPCCacheServerKey(
1794	            model_name=self.model_name,
1795	            world_size=self.world_size,
1796	            worker_id=self.worker_id,
1797	            token_ids=tuple(token_ids),
1798	            start=start,
1799	            end=end,
1800	            request_id=request_id,
1801	            cache_salt=cache_salt,
1802	        )
1803
